basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Māʾida — "The Table Spread" — is among the last sūras to be revealed, Medinan, and it opens with the great command of faithfulness: "Fulfil your covenants" (awfū bi-l-ʿuqūd). Qushayrī hears in it at once the echo of the primordial pact: God called them before He shaped them, named them believers before He created them; none attains what he hopes for save through the grace that went before him. The sūra then unfolds the lawful and the unlawful, ablution and purity, the equity of testimony, the demanding dialogue with the People of the Book, the story of Adam's two sons, and the Table sent down from heaven at the request of the apostles of ʿĪsā. At its heart shines the verse of mutual love — "He loves them and they love Him" — which Qushayrī glosses thus: His love for them precedes their love for Him; what you take to be your reaching toward Him is but the reflection of His choice that went before you. In verse 3 stands the seal: "Today I have perfected your religion for you." The sūra closes on the confession of ʿĪsā and the absolute sovereignty of God over the heavens and the earth.
themes: faithfulness to covenants and the primordial pact
the lawful and the unlawful, the purification of the heart
dialogue with the People of the Book
mutual love between God and His chosen
the Table sent down from heaven and the confession of ʿĪsā

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَوْفُوا بِالْعُقُودِ ۚ أُحِلَّتْ لَكُم بَهِيمَةُ الْأَنْعَامِ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ غَيْرَ مُحِلِّي الصَّيْدِ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū awfū bi-l-ʿuqūd uḥillat lakum bahīmatu l-anʿāmi illā mā yutlā ʿalaykum ghayra muḥillī ṣ-ṣaydi wa-antum ḥurum inna llāha yaḥkumu mā yurīd
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fulfil your covenants. The beasts of the flocks are made lawful to you, save those that are recited to you, with hunting forbidden while you are in the state of consecration. God decrees whatever He wills.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens the sūra with the echo of the primordial pact: God called them before He shaped them, named them believers before He created them. The very first address, "O you who believe," is an honour; the command "fulfil" is a burden — and the honour of praise always precedes the toil of obligation. The covenant (ʿaqd) is not only the word given to men, but what the heart has knotted, secret to secret, with its Lord. Faithfulness to the pact is sincere only in purity of intention, free of any claim to merit of one's own.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُحِلُّوا شَعَائِرَ اللَّهِ وَلَا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ وَلَا الْهَدْيَ وَلَا الْقَلَائِدَ وَلَا آمِّينَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرِضْوَانًا ۚ وَإِذَا حَلَلْتُمْ فَاصْطَادُوا ۚ وَلَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ أَن صَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَن تَعْتَدُوا ۘ وَتَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَلَا تَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tuḥillū shaʿāʾira llāhi wa-lā sh-shahra l-ḥarāma wa-lā l-hadya wa-lā l-qalāʾida wa-lā āmmīna l-bayta l-ḥarāma yabtaghūna faḍlan min rabbihim wa-riḍwānan wa-idhā ḥalaltum fa-ṣṭādū wa-lā yajrimannakum shanaʾānu qawmin an ṣaddūkum ʿani l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi an taʿtadū wa-taʿāwanū ʿalā l-birri wa-t-taqwā wa-lā taʿāwanū ʿalā l-ithmi wa-l-ʿudwāni wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not violate the sacred rites of God, nor the sacred month, nor the offerings, nor the garlanded beasts, nor those repairing to the Sacred House seeking bounty and good pleasure from their Lord. When you leave the consecrated state, you may hunt. Let not hatred of a people, because they barred you from the Sacred Mosque, lead you to transgress. Help one another toward piety and God-fearing, and do not help one another toward sin and enmity. Fear God: God is severe in retribution.
versets.commentaire: The "rites" (shaʿāʾir) are the landmarks of religion; to venerate and magnify them is the very essence of religion, and this is attained only through surrender when the decree descends upon us. Qushayrī distinguishes: righteousness (birr) is to do what is commanded; God-fearing (taqwā) is to abstain from what is forbidden. He warns that to help in sin is to set an act that others imitate: your conduct then becomes a custom that spreads, and its weight falls back upon you.

versets.ar: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ وَلَحْمُ الْخِنزِيرِ وَمَا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ وَالْمُنْخَنِقَةُ وَالْمَوْقُوذَةُ وَالْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ وَأَن تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلَامِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ فِسْقٌ ۗ الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن دِينِكُمْ فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَاخْشَوْنِ ۚ الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ دِينًا ۚ فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ فِي مَخْمَصَةٍ غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لِّإِثْمٍ ۙ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: ḥurrimat ʿalaykumu l-maytatu wa-d-damu wa-laḥmu l-khinzīri wa-mā uhilla li-ghayri llāhi bihi wa-l-munkhaniqatu wa-l-mawqūdhatu wa-l-mutaraddiyatu wa-n-naṭīḥatu wa-mā akala s-sabuʿu illā mā dhakkaytum wa-mā dhubiḥa ʿalā n-nuṣubi wa-an tastaqsimū bi-l-azlāmi dhālikum fisq al-yawma yaʾisa lladhīna kafarū min dīnikum fa-lā takhshawhum wa-khshawni l-yawma akmaltu lakum dīnakum wa-atmamtu ʿalaykum niʿmatī wa-raḍītu lakumu l-islāma dīnan fa-mani ḍṭurra fī makhmaṣatin ghayra mutajānifin li-ithmin fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Forbidden to you are: carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, what has been offered to other than God, the strangled beast, the one beaten to death, the one fallen from a height, the one gored, and what a wild beast has devoured — unless you have slaughtered it — and what has been sacrificed on stone altars, and that you divide by divining arrows: that is an act of disobedience. Today those who disbelieve despair of your religion; so fear them not, but fear Me. Today I have perfected your religion for you, completed My favour upon you, and approved Islam as your religion. But whoever is compelled by hunger, with no inclination to sin — God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: At the heart of the verse stands the seal: "Today I have perfected your religion for you." Qushayrī: to perfect the religion is to guard belief from any deficiency — God illumined the hearts of those who sought Him until He brought them to the fullness of knowledge, with no falling short. He also reads the dietary prohibitions as an inward parable: just as a beast slaughtered in God's name becomes lawful, so the one who has slain his soul beneath the knives of spiritual struggle and purified it — his nearness is lawful, his company is blessed.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ مَاذَا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ ۖ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ ۙ وَمَا عَلَّمْتُم مِّنَ الْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللَّهُ ۖ فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka mādhā uḥilla lahum qul uḥilla lakumu ṭ-ṭayyibātu wa-mā ʿallamtum mina l-jawāriḥi mukallibīna tuʿallimūnahunna mimmā ʿallamakumu llāhu fa-kulū mimmā amsakna ʿalaykum wa-dhkurū sma llāhi ʿalayhi wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha sarīʿu l-ḥisāb
versets.trad: They ask you what is made lawful to them. Say: lawful to you are the good things, and what you have taught the hunting beasts to catch, training them as God has taught you. So eat of what they catch for you, and pronounce God's name over it. Fear God: God is swift in reckoning.
versets.commentaire: The "good things" (ṭayyibāt), for Qushayrī, are not what souls find pleasant, but the lawful whose consumption begets clarity of heart, for to eat the unlawful hardens the heart, and desolation is the companion of hardness. The trained dog that holds its prey for its master becomes a sign: likewise the one whose acts and states are consecrated to God, unmixed with self-interest, sees his rank rise and his station exalted.

versets.ar: الْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتُ ۖ وَطَعَامُ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حِلٌّ لَّكُمْ وَطَعَامُكُمْ حِلٌّ لَّهُمْ ۖ وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ إِذَا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحِينَ وَلَا مُتَّخِذِي أَخْدَانٍ ۗ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِالْإِيمَانِ فَقَدْ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهُ وَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: al-yawma uḥilla lakumu ṭ-ṭayyibātu wa-ṭaʿāmu lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba ḥillun lakum wa-ṭaʿāmukum ḥillun lahum wa-l-muḥṣanātu mina l-muʾmināti wa-l-muḥṣanātu mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba min qablikum idhā ātaytumūhunna ujūrahunna muḥṣinīna ghayra musāfiḥīna wa-lā muttakhidhī akhdānin wa-man yakfur bi-l-īmāni fa-qad ḥabiṭa ʿamaluhu wa-huwa fī l-ākhirati mina l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: Today the good things are made lawful to you. The food of those given the Book is lawful to you, and your food is lawful to them. So too are the chaste women among the believers and the chaste women among those given the Book before you, when you give them their bridal due, as men in chastity, not in licence, nor taking secret lovers. Whoever rejects faith, his work comes to nothing, and in the Hereafter he is among the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī specifies: the good is not what the soul craves, but that in which the good pleasure of the Real is found — only then does the heart find its rest. The sharing of food with the People of the Book reflects the measure of accord that remains between us in affirming Lordship. But to bind one's heart to a secret companion is forbidden: if it is not sound to tie the heart even to a believer by way of hidden intimacy, how much less with those who stand apart?

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن كُنتُمْ جُنُبًا فَاطَّهَّرُوا ۚ وَإِن كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰ أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ أَوْ جَاءَ أَحَدٌ مِّنكُم مِّنَ الْغَائِطِ أَوْ لَامَسْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا مَاءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا صَعِيدًا طَيِّبًا فَامْسَحُوا بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُم مِّنْهُ ۚ مَا يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ لِيَجْعَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْ حَرَجٍ وَلَٰكِن يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā qumtum ilā ṣ-ṣalāti fa-ghsilū wujūhakum wa-aydiyakum ilā l-marāfiqi wa-msaḥū bi-ruʾūsikum wa-arjulakum ilā l-kaʿbayni wa-in kuntum junuban fa-ṭṭahharū wa-in kuntum marḍā aw ʿalā safarin aw jāʾa aḥadun minkum mina l-ghāʾiṭi aw lāmastumu n-nisāʾa fa-lam tajidū māʾan fa-tayammamū ṣaʿīdan ṭayyiban fa-msaḥū bi-wujūhikum wa-aydīkum minhu mā yurīdu llāhu li-yajʿala ʿalaykum min ḥarajin wa-lākin yurīdu li-yuṭahhirakum wa-li-yutimma niʿmatahu ʿalaykum laʿallakum tashkurūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you rise for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, wipe your heads, and wash your feet up to the ankles. If you are in a state of major impurity, purify yourselves. If you are ill, or on a journey, or one of you has come from the privy, or you have touched women and find no water, then betake yourselves to clean earth and wipe your faces and hands with it. God does not wish to lay any hardship upon you, but to purify you and to complete His favour upon you, that you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī overlays the ritual ablution with an ablution of the inmost: as the body is purified by the water of heaven, the heart is purified by the water of remorse and shame, then by the water of awe. To each washed limb corresponds an inward guarding: to wash the face is to keep it from abasing itself before others; to wash the hands is to restrain them from the unlawful; to wash the feet is to keep them from going where they ought not. And the dry ablution without water figures the disciple who, lacking a guide, drinks from the traces of those who went before.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَمِيثَاقَهُ الَّذِي وَاثَقَكُم بِهِ إِذْ قُلْتُمْ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: wa-dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum wa-mīthāqahu lladhī wāthaqakum bihi idh qultum samiʿnā wa-aṭaʿnā wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūr
versets.trad: And remember God's favour upon you and His covenant by which He bound you, when you said: We have heard and we have obeyed. Fear God: God knows what is within the breasts.
versets.commentaire: "Remember the covenant" refers, for Qushayrī, to the primordial pact sealed while they were still in nothingness: God named them believers and decreed their pardon before any disobedience existed. When He brought them forth, He taught them Oneness before imposing the limits, then set before them the trust and warned them against betrayal. "God knows what is within the breasts": nothing of the stirrings of your hearts escapes Him.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ لِلَّهِ شُهَدَاءَ بِالْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَآنُ قَوْمٍ عَلَىٰ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا ۚ اعْدِلُوا هُوَ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū kūnū qawwāmīna li-llāhi shuhadāʾa bi-l-qisṭi wa-lā yajrimannakum shanaʾānu qawmin ʿalā allā taʿdilū iʿdilū huwa aqrabu li-t-taqwā wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha khabīrun bimā taʿmalūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Be upright for God, witnesses in equity. Let not hatred of a people lead you to be unjust. Be just: that is nearer to God-fearing. Fear God: God is well aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: "Be upright for God": Qushayrī teaches that no personal interest should turn you from the faithfulness owed to the Real. He warns that the heart's grudges must never lead to injustice, for the pasture of oppression is poisoned and the places of deviation are deadly. And he adds that the truth of justice is fulfilled only by turning away from every personal share and gain.

versets.ar: وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ ۙ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: waʿada llāhu lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lahum maghfiratun wa-ajrun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a subtlety: forgiveness bears only upon fault; yet God first described them as people of righteous works, then promised them forgiveness — that we may know the servant can have good works and still bear sins that call for His mercy. Thus even the righteous remains suspended upon pardon: without it, he would perish.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jaḥīm
versets.trad: But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs — they are the companions of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses: they have two punishments — one brought forward, which is separation; the other deferred, which is burning. The pain of distance precedes and grounds the pain of the fire: to be veiled from the Beloved is the first of torments.
