mots.def: giving the lie to the verses.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ج ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ هَمَّ قَوْمٌ أَن يَبْسُطُوا إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ فَكَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum idh hamma qawmun an yabsuṭū ilaykum aydiyahum fa-kaffa aydiyahum ʿankum wa-ttaqū llāha wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Remember God's favour upon you, when a people resolved to stretch out their hands against you, and He held their hands back from you. Fear God: and in God let the believers place their trust.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes here the grace that wards off (dafʿ) from the grace that bestows (nafʿ): He recalls the favour of protection — He held the enemies' hands far from you. And he marvels at this unsolicited care: how lavishly were you favoured by One who watched over your unseen without your asking, with no intercession on your behalf, with no hope of any profit He might draw from you.

mots.racine: ه م م
ب س ط
mots.sens: to stretch their hands against you
mots.def: to extend the hand to harm.

mots.racine: و ك ل

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ وَبَعَثْنَا مِنْهُمُ اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ نَقِيبًا ۖ وَقَالَ اللَّهُ إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ ۖ لَئِنْ أَقَمْتُمُ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَيْتُمُ الزَّكَاةَ وَآمَنتُم بِرُسُلِي وَعَزَّرْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَقْرَضْتُمُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا لَّأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنكُمْ سَيِّئَاتِكُمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ۚ فَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ مِنكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: wa-laqad akhadha llāhu mīthāqa banī isrāʾīla wa-baʿathnā minhumu thnay ʿashara naqīban wa-qāla llāhu innī maʿakum la-in aqamtumu ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātaytumu z-zakāta wa-āmantum bi-rusulī wa-ʿazzartumūhum wa-aqraḍtumu llāha qarḍan ḥasanan la-ukaffiranna ʿankum sayyiʾātikum wa-la-udkhilannakum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru fa-man kafara baʿda dhālika minkum fa-qad ḍalla sawāʾa s-sabīl
versets.trad: God took a covenant from the Children of Israel, and We raised up from among them twelve chiefs. And God said: I am with you. If you establish the prayer, give the alms, believe in My messengers and support them, and lend to God a goodly loan, I shall surely efface your evil deeds and admit you into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. But whoever among you disbelieves after that has strayed from the level path.
themes: the covenant of the Children of Israel
versets.commentaire: "I am with you": Qushayrī hears it as a reciprocal promise — if you fulfil My right, I will bring you your portions; if you magnify My command in the fleeting, I will exalt your rank in the everlasting. He then distinguishes two givings: the rich spend their wealth on God's path; the poor give their souls and their very breath in seeking God. The first set aside five of two hundred dirhams; the second withhold from His command neither a breath nor an atom.

mots.racine: ن ق ب
ع ز ر
ق ر ض / ح س ن

versets.ar: فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِم مِّيثَاقَهُمْ لَعَنَّاهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَا قُلُوبَهُمْ قَاسِيَةً ۖ يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ عَن مَّوَاضِعِهِ ۙ وَنَسُوا حَظًّا مِّمَّا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ ۚ وَلَا تَزَالُ تَطَّلِعُ عَلَىٰ خَائِنَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۖ فَاعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَاصْفَحْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-bimā naqḍihim mīthāqahum laʿannāhum wa-jaʿalnā qulūbahum qāsiyatan yuḥarrifūna l-kalima ʿan mawāḍiʿihi wa-nasū ḥaẓẓan mimmā dhukkirū bihi wa-lā tazālu taṭṭaliʿu ʿalā khāʾinatin minhum illā qalīlan minhum fa-ʿfu ʿanhum wa-ṣfaḥ inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: Because they broke their covenant We cursed them and hardened their hearts. They distort the words from their places and have forgotten part of what they were reminded of. You will not cease to discover treachery among them, save a few. Yet pardon them and overlook: God loves those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī formulates a law of the heart: the wage of disobedience was their abandonment to themselves, and that abandonment increases disobedience. Hardness of heart is no arbitrary fate but the fruit of breaking the pact. And yet the command falls: "pardon them and overlook" — for God loves those who do good, and magnanimity toward the offender is the mark of beneficence.

mots.racine: ن ق ض / و ث ق
ق ل ب / ق س و
ح ر ف / ك ل م
ع ف و / ص ف ح

versets.ar: وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّا نَصَارَىٰ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَهُمْ فَنَسُوا حَظًّا مِّمَّا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ فَأَغْرَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ ۚ وَسَوْفَ يُنَبِّئُهُمُ اللَّهُ بِمَا كَانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mina lladhīna qālū innā naṣārā akhadhnā mīthāqahum fa-nasū ḥaẓẓan mimmā dhukkirū bihi fa-aghraynā baynahumu l-ʿadāwata wa-l-baghḍāʾa ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati wa-sawfa yunabbiʾuhumu llāhu bimā kānū yaṣnaʿūn
versets.trad: And from those who say, We are Christians, We took their covenant; but they forgot part of what they were reminded of, so We stirred up among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection. God will inform them of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī teaches that forgetting part of the reminder leads to dispersion: whoever neglects his share of the pact is given over to discord. The enmity that settles among them is but the outward reflection of an inward rupture: to have forgotten the covenant with God is to lose the concord between hearts.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
ن س ي / ح ظ ظ
ع د و / ب غ ض

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ قَدْ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنَا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا كُنتُمْ تُخْفُونَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَيَعْفُو عَن كَثِيرٍ ۚ قَدْ جَاءَكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ نُورٌ وَكِتَابٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi qad jāʾakum rasūlunā yubayyinu lakum kathīran mimmā kuntum tukhfūna mina l-kitābi wa-yaʿfū ʿan kathīrin qad jāʾakum mina llāhi nūrun wa-kitābun mubīn
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Our Messenger has come to you, making clear to you much of what you used to conceal of the Scripture, and passing over much. There has come to you from God a light and a clear Book.
versets.commentaire: "There has come to you a light from God": Qushayrī sees in this light the very radiance of guidance that unveils what hearts had buried. The Real unveils what He wills and veils what He wills — the Messenger makes clear "much" and passes over the rest, in mercy, for the whole revelation would consume one not ready to receive it.

mots.racine: ء ه ل / ك ت ب
خ ف ي
mots.sens: you used to conceal of the Scripture

mots.racine: ن و ر / ب ي ن
mots.sens: a light and a clear Book

versets.ar: يَهْدِي بِهِ اللَّهُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ رِضْوَانَهُ سُبُلَ السَّلَامِ وَيُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ بِإِذْنِهِ وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: yahdī bihi llāhu mani ttabaʿa riḍwānahu subula s-salāmi wa-yukhrijuhum mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri bi-idhnihi wa-yahdīhim ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīm
versets.trad: By it God guides those who seek His good pleasure to the ways of peace, and brings them out of the darknesses into the light by His leave, and guides them to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the ways of salvation are many yet converge upon the One; whoever seeks the divine good pleasure, God leads him out of the darknesses of the soul into the light of the spirit. The going-out "by His leave" means that no step toward the light is taken by the servant's will alone: it is He who brings out, He who guides.

mots.racine: س ب ل / س ل م
ظ ل م / ن و ر
ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۚ قُلْ فَمَن يَمْلِكُ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا إِنْ أَرَادَ أَن يُهْلِكَ الْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ وَمَن فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ۗ وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: laqad kafara lladhīna qālū inna llāha huwa l-masīḥu bnu maryama qul fa-man yamliku mina llāhi shayʾan in arāda an yuhlika l-masīḥa bna maryama wa-ummahu wa-man fī l-arḍi jamīʿan wa-li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā baynahumā yakhluqu mā yashāʾu wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: They have disbelieved who say: God is the Messiah, son of Mary. Say: who then could hold anything against God if He willed to destroy the Messiah, son of Mary, and his mother, and everyone on earth? To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and what is between them. He creates what He wills, and God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī stresses the absolute rupture between Creator and creature: one subject to birth and death, who eats and can be annihilated, cannot be the Real; the Creator is bounded by nothing and none can hold anything against Him. "He creates what He wills" closes off every claim to enclose divinity in a form: His sovereignty embraces the heavens, the earth, and their between.

mots.racine: م س ح
م ل ك
mots.sens: who could hold anything against God
mots.def: none disposes of aught against Him.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ش ي ء

versets.ar: وَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ وَالنَّصَارَىٰ نَحْنُ أَبْنَاءُ اللَّهِ وَأَحِبَّاؤُهُ ۚ قُلْ فَلِمَ يُعَذِّبُكُم بِذُنُوبِكُم ۖ بَلْ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ مِّمَّنْ خَلَقَ ۚ يَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-qālati l-yahūdu wa-n-naṣārā naḥnu abnāʾu llāhi wa-aḥibbāʾuhu qul fa-lima yuʿadhdhibukum bi-dhunūbikum bal antum basharun mimman khalaqa yaghfiru li-man yashāʾu wa-yuʿadhdhibu man yashāʾu wa-li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā baynahumā wa-ilayhi l-maṣīr
versets.trad: The Jews and the Christians say: We are the sons of God and His beloved. Say: then why does He punish you for your sins? No, you are mortals among those He created. He forgives whom He wills and punishes whom He wills. To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, and to Him is the final return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the false familiarity: to believe oneself beloved without the ransom of trial is illusion; true love of God is known by what it endures for Him, not by what it claims. Were one truly a son and beloved, one would not be punished for one's sins. "To Him is the return": all that came from Him ends by returning to Him.

mots.racine: ب ن و / ح ب ب
mots.sens: sons of God and His beloved

mots.racine: ب ش ر / خ ل ق
ص ي ر
mots.def: the final term of all things.

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ قَدْ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنَا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ عَلَىٰ فَتْرَةٍ مِّنَ الرُّسُلِ أَن تَقُولُوا مَا جَاءَنَا مِن بَشِيرٍ وَلَا نَذِيرٍ ۖ فَقَدْ جَاءَكُم بَشِيرٌ وَنَذِيرٌ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi qad jāʾakum rasūlunā yubayyinu lakum ʿalā fatratin mina r-rusuli an taqūlū mā jāʾanā min bashīrin wa-lā nadhīrin fa-qad jāʾakum bashīrun wa-nadhīrun wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Our Messenger has come to make things clear to you, after an interval in the messengers, lest you should say: No bearer of glad tidings nor warner has come to us. Now there has come to you a bearer of glad tidings and a warner. God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unites in the person of the Messenger the two faces of providence: glad tidings (bishāra) for those whom grace draws, and warning (nidhāra) for those whom justice threatens. The "fatra," the interval without a prophet, makes the mercy of this coming shine forth: God never leaves His servants without a reminder, lest they have an excuse.

mots.racine: ف ت ر
ب ش ر
ن ذ ر

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ يَا قَوْمِ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَعَلَ فِيكُمْ أَنبِيَاءَ وَجَعَلَكُم مُّلُوكًا وَآتَاكُم مَّا لَمْ يُؤْتِ أَحَدًا مِّنَ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla mūsā li-qawmihi yā qawmi dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum idh jaʿala fīkum anbiyāʾa wa-jaʿalakum mulūkan wa-ātākum mā lam yuʾti aḥadan mina l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Remember the words of Moses to his people: O my people! Remember God's favour upon you, when He raised up prophets among you, made you kings, and gave you what He had given to no other in all the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Moses calls his people to remember the favours, and Qushayrī makes remembrance (dhikr) the root of all gratitude: whoever forgets the gift forgets the Giver, and whoever remembers it is led from the gift to the Giver. Prophethood and kingship are but loans: the true favour is not what sets you apart from men, but what draws you near to God.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
ج ع ل / ن ب ء
م ل ك

versets.ar: يَا قَوْمِ ادْخُلُوا الْأَرْضَ الْمُقَدَّسَةَ الَّتِي كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَلَا تَرْتَدُّوا عَلَىٰ أَدْبَارِكُمْ فَتَنقَلِبُوا خَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā qawmi dkhulū l-arḍa l-muqaddasata llatī kataba llāhu lakum wa-lā tartaddū ʿalā adbārikum fa-tanqalibū khāsirīn
versets.trad: O my people! Enter the holy land which God has decreed for you, and do not turn back, or you will become losers.
themes: the command not to turn back
versets.commentaire: "Enter the holy land": Qushayrī hears the call to enter the dwellings of the heart's sanctification. To turn back is to forsake the path after setting foot upon it — and none turns back without becoming a loser. The promised land figures the unveiling promised to one who advances without looking behind.

mots.racine: ء ر ض / ق د س
ك ت ب
ر د د / د ب ر

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا مُوسَىٰ إِنَّ فِيهَا قَوْمًا جَبَّارِينَ وَإِنَّا لَن نَّدْخُلَهَا حَتَّىٰ يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهَا فَإِن يَخْرُجُوا مِنْهَا فَإِنَّا دَاخِلُونَ
versets.tr: qālū yā mūsā inna fīhā qawman jabbārīna wa-innā lan nadkhulahā ḥattā yakhrujū minhā fa-in yakhrujū minhā fa-innā dākhilūn
versets.trad: They said: O Moses! In it is a people of fierce strength; we shall not enter it until they leave. If they leave, then we shall enter.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in this refusal the image of the disciple who sets conditions upon his commitment: he wants the station without paying its price, the arrival without the crossing. To invoke the adversary's strength as a reason to defer obedience is to ignore that the One who commands entry is also the One who grants victory.

mots.racine: ج ب ر
د خ ل
خ ر ج

versets.ar: قَالَ رَجُلَانِ مِنَ الَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمَا ادْخُلُوا عَلَيْهِمُ الْبَابَ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُمُوهُ فَإِنَّكُمْ غَالِبُونَ ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوا إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rajulāni mina lladhīna yakhāfūna anʿama llāhu ʿalayhimā dkhulū ʿalayhimu l-bāba fa-idhā dakhaltumūhu fa-innakum ghālibūna wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-tawakkalū in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: Two men of those who feared God, whom God had favoured, said: Enter upon them through the gate; once you have entered, you will be victorious. And in God place your trust, if you are believers.
themes: the faith of the two men
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī highlights the bond between reverent fear and grace: those who fear God have received His favour, and that favour lets them see victory where others see only menace. "Place your trust in God, if you are believers": trust is the very measure of faith; whoever leans upon God is never abandoned.

mots.racine: خ و ف
د خ ل / ب و ب
mots.sens: enter upon them through the gate

mots.racine: و ك ل

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا مُوسَىٰ إِنَّا لَن نَّدْخُلَهَا أَبَدًا مَّا دَامُوا فِيهَا ۖ فَاذْهَبْ أَنتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقَاتِلَا إِنَّا هَاهُنَا قَاعِدُونَ
versets.tr: qālū yā mūsā innā lan nadkhulahā abadan mā dāmū fīhā fa-dhhab anta wa-rabbuka fa-qātilā innā hāhunā qāʿidūn
versets.trad: They said: O Moses! We shall never enter it so long as they remain. So go, you and your Lord, and fight; we shall sit here.
versets.commentaire: The words are of extreme insolence, and Qushayrī sees in them the height of the soul that shirks: it dismisses the duty and seeks its ease, leaving the struggle to another. It is the stance of one who wants the fruit of effort without the effort, the gift without the toil — and the servant's toil is the seal of the sincerity of his giving.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ء ب د
ذ ه ب
ق ع د

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي لَا أَمْلِكُ إِلَّا نَفْسِي وَأَخِي ۖ فَافْرُقْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ الْقَوْمِ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi innī lā amliku illā nafsī wa-akhī fa-fruq baynanā wa-bayna l-qawmi l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: He said: My Lord! I have power over none but myself and my brother. So part us from this disobedient people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī admires the prophet's humility: he confesses his powerlessness and commits the matter to his Lord, for the true servant claims no power apart from God. To acknowledge "I have power over none but myself" is the summit of spiritual poverty (faqr): all the rest belongs to the Master.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ن ف س
mots.sens: I have power over none but myself

mots.racine: ف ر ق
ف س ق

versets.ar: قَالَ فَإِنَّهَا مُحَرَّمَةٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ۛ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً ۛ يَتِيهُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ فَلَا تَأْسَ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla fa-innahā muḥarramatun ʿalayhim arbaʿīna sanatan yatīhūna fī l-arḍi fa-lā taʾsa ʿalā l-qawmi l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: He said: Then it is forbidden to them for forty years, during which they shall wander in the land. So do not grieve over this disobedient people.
versets.commentaire: The wandering in the desert is, for Qushayrī, the punishment of those who refuse to enter when the command comes: whoever does not advance at the hour of the call is lost in the wildernesses of doubt. "Do not grieve" is a consolation addressed to the heart of the guide: grief for those who refuse must not veil the peace of the one who transmits.

mots.racine: ح ر م
ت ي ه
ء س و

versets.ar: وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ابْنَيْ آدَمَ بِالْحَقِّ إِذْ قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا فَتُقُبِّلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمَا وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنَ الْآخَرِ قَالَ لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-tlu ʿalayhim nabaʾa bnay ādama bi-l-ḥaqqi idh qarrabā qurbānan fa-tuqubbila min aḥadihimā wa-lam yutaqabbal mina l-ākhari qāla la-aqtulannaka qāla innamā yataqabbalu llāhu mina l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Recite to them, in truth, the tale of Adam's two sons: when they each offered a sacrifice, and it was accepted from one and not from the other. He said: I will surely kill you. The other said: God accepts only from the God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lays all the weight on acceptance: the offering has worth only by the state of the heart that offers it; God accepts only from one who fears Him, for it is the purity of intent, not the appearance of the gift, that rises to Him. The envy of the rejected brother is born of his gazing at the gift rather than at his own heart.

mots.racine: ن ب ء / ب ن و
mots.sens: the tale of Adam's two sons

mots.racine: ق ر ب
ق ب ل / و ق ي

versets.ar: لَئِن بَسَطتَ إِلَيَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِي مَا أَنَا بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِيَ إِلَيْكَ لِأَقْتُلَكَ ۖ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: la-in basaṭta ilayya yadaka li-taqtulanī mā ana bi-bāsiṭin yadiya ilayka li-aqtulaka innī akhāfu llāha rabba l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: If you stretch out your hand to kill me, I will not stretch out my hand to kill you; I fear God, the Lord of the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī praises the restraint of the just one: he does not retaliate, not from weakness, but from reverent fear of God — the fear of the Lord of the worlds outweighs the instinct of self-defence. Not stretching out the hand toward blood is here the fruit of an inward presence: whoever sees God in all things withholds his hand from all harm.

mots.racine: ب س ط
mots.sens: you stretch your hand against me
mots.def: to extend the hand to kill.

mots.racine: ب س ط
mots.sens: I will not stretch my hand

mots.racine: خ و ف / ر ب ب
mots.sens: I fear God, Lord of the worlds

versets.ar: إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَن تَبُوءَ بِإِثْمِي وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: innī urīdu an tabūʾa bi-ithmī wa-ithmika fa-takūna min aṣḥābi n-nāri wa-dhālika jazāʾu ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: I would rather you bear my sin and your own, and become one of the companions of the Fire; for that is the recompense of the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the just one would sooner bear the wrong than commit it; he prefers to be wronged than to be a wronger. To wish that the other bear "my sin and your own" is no curse but the avowal that he himself will not take up the crime — he leaves to the aggressor the responsibility of his own deed, knowing the Fire is the wrongdoer's portion.

mots.racine: ب و ء / ء ث م
mots.sens: you bear my sin and yours

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر
ج ز ي / ظ ل م

versets.ar: فَطَوَّعَتْ لَهُ نَفْسُهُ قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ فَقَتَلَهُ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ṭawwaʿat lahu nafsuhu qatla akhīhi fa-qatalahu fa-aṣbaḥa mina l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: His soul prompted him to kill his brother, so he killed him and became one of the losers.
versets.commentaire: "His soul prompted him": Qushayrī here marks the nafs as the faculty that dresses evil in allure. The soul makes easy what leads to ruin and makes transgression seem fair; whoever yields to it becomes a loser in that very instant. The first blood shed is born of a soul that has taken precedence over the heart.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ن ف س
ق ت ل / ء خ و
خ س ر

versets.ar: فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ غُرَابًا يَبْحَثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيُرِيَهُ كَيْفَ يُوَارِي سَوْءَةَ أَخِيهِ ۚ قَالَ يَا وَيْلَتَا أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَٰذَا الْغُرَابِ فَأُوَارِيَ سَوْءَةَ أَخِي ۖ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ النَّادِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-baʿatha llāhu ghurāban yabḥathu fī l-arḍi li-yuriyahu kayfa yuwārī sawʾata akhīhi qāla yā waylatā a-ʿajaztu an akūna mithla hādhā l-ghurābi fa-uwāriya sawʾata akhī fa-aṣbaḥa mina n-nādimīn
versets.trad: Then God sent a crow scratching in the earth, to show him how to hide his brother's corpse. He said: Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and hide my brother's corpse? And he became one of the remorseful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the irony of the teaching: man, endowed with reason, had to be instructed by a bird — a humbling for one who forsook guidance and let his passion lead him. Remorse (nadam) comes too late to undo the crime, yet it is the first step of repentance: the regret born in the heart is already a summons of mercy.

mots.racine: غ ر ب / ب ح ث
و ر ي / س و ء
ن د م

versets.ar: مِنْ أَجْلِ ذَٰلِكَ كَتَبْنَا عَلَىٰ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ أَنَّهُ مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا وَمَنْ أَحْيَاهَا فَكَأَنَّمَا أَحْيَا النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي الْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ
versets.tr: min ajli dhālika katabnā ʿalā banī isrāʾīla annahu man qatala nafsan bi-ghayri nafsin aw fasādin fī l-arḍi fa-ka-annamā qatala n-nāsa jamīʿan wa-man aḥyāhā fa-ka-annamā aḥyā n-nāsa jamīʿan wa-laqad jāʾathum rusulunā bi-l-bayyināti thumma inna kathīran minhum baʿda dhālika fī l-arḍi la-musrifūn
versets.trad: For this reason We decreed for the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul — unless for a soul or for corruption in the land — it is as though he had killed all mankind; and whoever saves a life, it is as though he had saved all mankind. Our messengers came to them with clear proofs, yet many of them, after that, were transgressors in the land.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates this vertigo: a single soul is worth all souls, for in each one all humanity is reflected; to kill one life unjustly is to assault the sacred trust God has confided. And to save a life is to share in the vivifying work of the Living. The crime or the saving of one alone thus rebounds upon the whole.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ف س
ن ف س / ف س د
mots.sens: not for a soul or corruption

mots.racine: ح ي ي

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا جَزَاءُ الَّذِينَ يُحَارِبُونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَسَادًا أَن يُقَتَّلُوا أَوْ يُصَلَّبُوا أَوْ تُقَطَّعَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُم مِّنْ خِلَافٍ أَوْ يُنفَوْا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لَهُمْ خِزْيٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā jazāʾu lladhīna yuḥāribūna llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-yasʿawna fī l-arḍi fasādan an yuqattalū aw yuṣallabū aw tuqaṭṭaʿa aydīhim wa-arjuluhum min khilāfin aw yunfaw mina l-arḍi dhālika lahum khizyun fī d-dunyā wa-lahum fī l-ākhirati ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: The recompense of those who make war on God and His Messenger and strive to spread corruption in the land is only that they be killed, or crucified, or have their hands and feet cut off on opposite sides, or be banished from the land. That is their disgrace in this world, and in the Hereafter theirs is a terrible punishment,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in "war against God" the supreme image of inward rebellion: whoever spreads corruption in the land first bears corruption within his own heart. The disgrace of this world is but the shadow cast by spiritual veiling: distance from God is the true punishment, of which the visible penalties are only the reflection.

mots.racine: ح ر ب
mots.sens: make war on God and His Messenger

mots.racine: س ع ي / ف س د
خ ز ي

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَقْدِرُوا عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna tābū min qabli an taqdirū ʿalayhim fa-ʿlamū anna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Except those who repent before you overpower them. Know that God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes repentance an ever-open door: mercy outruns justice; whoever returns before the hand of punishment closes finds a Forgiving, Compassionate One. Sincere repentance, accomplished before capture, abolishes the penalty — a sign that God prefers His servant's return to his punishment.

mots.racine: ت و ب
ق د ر
غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَابْتَغُوا إِلَيْهِ الْوَسِيلَةَ وَجَاهِدُوا فِي سَبِيلِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-btaghū ilayhi l-wasīlata wa-jāhidū fī sabīlihi laʿallakum tufliḥūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fear God, seek the means of approach to Him, and strive in His path, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: "Seek the means (wasīla) of approach to Him": Qushayrī sees in this the heart of the way. The means to God is approach through acts of obedience and the consuming of the soul in love; none draws near to Him save through Him. The struggle (jihād) he speaks of is first that of the soul against its inclinations, the condition of true prosperity.

mots.racine: و ق ي
ب غ ي / و س ل
ج ه د / س ب ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُم مَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لِيَفْتَدُوا بِهِ مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ مَا تُقُبِّلَ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū law anna lahum mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan wa-mithlahu maʿahu li-yaftadū bihi min ʿadhābi yawmi l-qiyāmati mā tuqubbila minhum wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: As for those who disbelieve, even if they possessed all that is on the earth, and the like of it with it, to ransom themselves thereby from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it would not be accepted from them. Theirs is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the lesson: no wealth ransoms one on the Day of Reckoning; what saves is not what the hand possesses but what the heart has loved. On the Day of Resurrection the gold of the whole world is worth nothing before the one coin that counts: faith and the love of God, which the disbeliever refused.

mots.racine: ء ر ض
ف د ي
ع ذ ب / ء ل م

versets.ar: يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَخْرُجُوا مِنَ النَّارِ وَمَا هُم بِخَارِجِينَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ
versets.tr: yurīdūna an yakhrujū mina n-nāri wa-mā hum bi-khārijīna minhā wa-lahum ʿadhābun muqīm
versets.trad: They will wish to come out of the Fire, but they will not come out of it; theirs is a lasting punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to desire escape without the power to escape is the height of anguish; they are held by what they chose in this world. The "lasting punishment" figures the definitive veiling: having refused the light when it was offered, they remain in the night they themselves summoned.

mots.racine: ر و د / خ ر ج
mots.sens: they will wish to come out

mots.racine: خ ر ج
ع ذ ب / ق و م

versets.ar: وَالسَّارِقُ وَالسَّارِقَةُ فَاقْطَعُوا أَيْدِيَهُمَا جَزَاءً بِمَا كَسَبَا نَكَالًا مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-s-sāriqu wa-s-sāriqatu fa-qṭaʿū aydiyahumā jazāʾan bimā kasabā nakālan mina llāhi wa-llāhu ʿazīzun ḥakīm
versets.trad: As for the thief, male and female, cut off their hands as a recompense for what they have earned, an exemplary punishment from God. God is Mighty, Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī joins the rigour of the penalty to divine wisdom: the punishment is not vengeance but order, and God, the Mighty the Wise, decrees nothing that is not just. The hand that reached toward the unlawful is cut off — an image, for the initiate, of every faculty turned from God that must be severed from its forbidden object.

mots.racine: س ر ق
ق ط ع
ن ك ل

versets.ar: فَمَن تَابَ مِن بَعْدِ ظُلْمِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-man tāba min baʿdi ẓulmihi wa-aṣlaḥa fa-inna llāha yatūbu ʿalayhi inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and amends, God will surely turn to him. God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī savours the reciprocity of the verb tawba: the servant turns back (tāba) and the Lord turns to him (yatūbu ʿalayhi) — His turning precedes and welcomes our own. Repentance is complete only with amendment (iṣlāḥ): to turn from evil and to mend; then mercy bends down and effaces.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ظ ل م
ص ل ح
ت و ب

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-lam taʿlam anna llāha lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yuʿadhdhibu man yashāʾu wa-yaghfiru li-man yashāʾu wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: Do you not know that to God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whom He wills and forgives whom He wills. God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī stresses the sovereign freedom: none has a claim upon God; He punishes by justice and forgives by grace, and both alike belong to His decision alone. To acknowledge this dominion is to cease demanding and learn to receive: all that comes from Him is favour, even severity.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: His is the dominion of heavens and earth

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ش ي ء
غ ف ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ لَا يَحْزُنكَ الَّذِينَ يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ مِنَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا آمَنَّا بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَلَمْ تُؤْمِن قُلُوبُهُمْ ۛ وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ هَادُوا ۛ سَمَّاعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ سَمَّاعُونَ لِقَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ لَمْ يَأْتُوكَ ۖ يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ مِن بَعْدِ مَوَاضِعِهِ ۖ يَقُولُونَ إِنْ أُوتِيتُمْ هَٰذَا فَخُذُوهُ وَإِن لَّمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ فَاحْذَرُوا ۚ وَمَن يُرِدِ اللَّهُ فِتْنَتَهُ فَلَن تَمْلِكَ لَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُرِدِ اللَّهُ أَن يُطَهِّرَ قُلُوبَهُمْ ۚ لَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا خِزْيٌ ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā r-rasūlu lā yaḥzunka lladhīna yusāriʿūna fī l-kufri mina lladhīna qālū āmannā bi-afwāhihim wa-lam tuʾmin qulūbuhum wa-mina lladhīna hādū sammāʿūna li-l-kadhibi sammāʿūna li-qawmin ākharīna lam yaʾtūka yuḥarrifūna l-kalima min baʿdi mawāḍiʿihi yaqūlūna in ūtītum hādhā fa-khudhūhu wa-in lam tuʾtawhu fa-ḥdharū wa-man yuridi llāhu fitnatahu fa-lan tamlika lahu mina llāhi shayʾan ulāʾika lladhīna lam yuridi llāhu an yuṭahhira qulūbahum lahum fī d-dunyā khizyun wa-lahum fī l-ākhirati ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: O Messenger! Let not those grieve you who hasten into disbelief — those who say with their mouths, We believe, while their hearts do not believe; and among the Jews, those who listen eagerly to falsehood, listening for other folk who have not come to you. They distort the words from their places, saying: If you are given this, take it; if not, beware. Whomever God wills to test, you can do nothing for him against God. Those are they whose hearts God does not will to purify; theirs is disgrace in this world, and in the Hereafter a terrible punishment.
themes: faith of the lips without the heart
versets.commentaire: "Let it not grieve you": Qushayrī hears a gentleness addressed to the Prophet's heart. The grief of the guide is born of love for the creatures; but the decree belongs to God, and none can purify a heart that God has not willed to purify. Faith of the lips without that of the heart is the great contrast: true belief is the conforming of the heart to what is announced of the unseen.

mots.racine: ح ز ن
س ر ع / ك ف ر
س م ع / ك ذ ب

mots.ar: لَمْ يُرِدِ اللَّهُ أَن يُطَهِّرَ قُلُوبَهُمْ
mots.tr: lam yuridi llāhu an yuṭahhira qulūbahum
mots.racine: ط ه ر / ق ل ب
mots.sens: God did not will to purify their hearts

versets.ar: سَمَّاعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ أَكَّالُونَ لِلسُّحْتِ ۚ فَإِن جَاءُوكَ فَاحْكُم بَيْنَهُمْ أَوْ أَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ فَلَن يَضُرُّوكَ شَيْئًا ۖ وَإِنْ حَكَمْتَ فَاحْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِالْقِسْطِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ
versets.tr: sammāʿūna li-l-kadhibi akkālūna li-s-suḥti fa-in jāʾūka fa-ḥkum baynahum aw aʿriḍ ʿanhum wa-in tuʿriḍ ʿanhum fa-lan yaḍurrūka shayʾan wa-in ḥakamta fa-ḥkum baynahum bi-l-qisṭi inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muqsiṭīn
versets.trad: Listeners to falsehood, devourers of unlawful gain. If they come to you, judge between them or turn away from them. If you turn away from them, they cannot harm you at all. But if you judge, judge between them with equity. God loves those who act justly.
versets.commentaire: "God loves those who act justly": Qushayrī makes equity (qisṭ) an object of divine love. Whoever renders justice even to his enemy attains a station of acceptance, for disinterested justice is the reflection of the divine balance. Unlawful gain (suḥt), by contrast, hardens and veils the heart: what one devours of the unlawful darkens the inner mirror.

mots.racine: ء ك ل / س ح ت
ح ك م / ق س ط
ح ب ب / ق س ط

versets.ar: وَكَيْفَ يُحَكِّمُونَكَ وَعِندَهُمُ التَّوْرَاةُ فِيهَا حُكْمُ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَا أُولَٰئِكَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-kayfa yuḥakkimūnaka wa-ʿindahumu t-tawrātu fīhā ḥukmu llāhi thumma yatawallawna min baʿdi dhālika wa-mā ulāʾika bi-l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: How is it that they take you for judge, when they have the Torah, in which is God's judgement, and then turn away after that? Those are not believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the inconstancy: they seek judgement where they hope for their advantage, and turn from it as soon as it crosses them. To possess the Book of judgement and to forsake it is to betray the trust deposited — true faith does not pick within the truth what suits it.

mots.racine: ح ك م
ح ك م
mots.def: the divine verdict in the Torah.

mots.racine: و ل ي

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَا التَّوْرَاةَ فِيهَا هُدًى وَنُورٌ ۚ يَحْكُمُ بِهَا النَّبِيُّونَ الَّذِينَ أَسْلَمُوا لِلَّذِينَ هَادُوا وَالرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبَارُ بِمَا اسْتُحْفِظُوا مِن كِتَابِ اللَّهِ وَكَانُوا عَلَيْهِ شُهَدَاءَ ۚ فَلَا تَخْشَوُا النَّاسَ وَاخْشَوْنِ وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا بِآيَاتِي ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: innā anzalnā t-tawrāta fīhā hudan wa-nūrun yaḥkumu bihā n-nabiyyūna lladhīna aslamū li-lladhīna hādū wa-r-rabbāniyyūna wa-l-aḥbāru bimā stuḥfiẓū min kitābi llāhi wa-kānū ʿalayhi shuhadāʾa fa-lā takhshawu n-nāsa wa-khshawni wa-lā tashtarū bi-āyātī thamanan qalīlan wa-man lam yaḥkum bimā anzala llāhu fa-ulāʾika humu l-kāfirūn
versets.trad: We sent down the Torah, in which is guidance and light, by which the prophets who had submitted judged for the Jews, as did the masters and the rabbis, by what they were entrusted to keep of God's Book, of which they were witnesses. So fear not men, but fear Me, and do not sell My signs for a paltry price. Those who do not judge by what God has sent down — they are the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: "Fear not men, fear Me": Qushayrī makes this word the pivot of sincerity. Whoever is certain that no benefit nor harm befalls save by the decision of the Real can harbour toward a creature neither desire nor fear. To sell God's signs for a paltry price is to trade the eternal for the fleeting — the worst of bargains.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ن و ر
mots.sens: in which is guidance and light

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ح ب ر
ش ر ي / ث م ن
mots.sens: do not sell My signs for a paltry price

versets.ar: وَكَتَبْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِيهَا أَنَّ النَّفْسَ بِالنَّفْسِ وَالْعَيْنَ بِالْعَيْنِ وَالْأَنفَ بِالْأَنفِ وَالْأُذُنَ بِالْأُذُنِ وَالسِّنَّ بِالسِّنِّ وَالْجُرُوحَ قِصَاصٌ ۚ فَمَن تَصَدَّقَ بِهِ فَهُوَ كَفَّارَةٌ لَّهُ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-katabnā ʿalayhim fīhā anna n-nafsa bi-n-nafsi wa-l-ʿayna bi-l-ʿayni wa-l-anfa bi-l-anfi wa-l-udhuna bi-l-udhuni wa-s-sinna bi-s-sinni wa-l-jurūḥa qiṣāṣun fa-man taṣaddaqa bihi fa-huwa kaffāratun lahu wa-man lam yaḥkum bimā anzala llāhu fa-ulāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: We prescribed for them in it: a life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth, and for wounds, retaliation. Whoever remits it as charity, it shall be an expiation for him. Those who do not judge by what God has sent down — they are the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the higher way: whoever forgoes his right, giving alms of his vengeance, gains for himself an expiation — the pardon granted to another becomes the ransom of his own faults. Retaliation is justice; magnanimous forgoing is excellence (iḥsān), which rises above the strict requital.

mots.racine: ن ف س
ج ر ح / ق ص ص
ص د ق

versets.ar: وَقَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰ آثَارِهِم بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ ۖ وَآتَيْنَاهُ الْإِنجِيلَ فِيهِ هُدًى وَنُورٌ وَمُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَهُدًى وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qaffaynā ʿalā āthārihim bi-ʿīsā bni maryama muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayhi mina t-tawrāti wa-ātaynāhu l-injīla fīhi hudan wa-nūrun wa-muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayhi mina t-tawrāti wa-hudan wa-mawʿiẓatan li-l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: We sent, following in their footsteps, Jesus son of Mary, confirming the Torah that came before him, and We gave him the Gospel, in which is guidance and light, confirming the Torah that came before, a guidance and admonition for the God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in this succession the unity of the prophetic thread: each messenger confirms the one before him, for the light is one, whether it shines in the Torah or in the Gospel. The "guidance and admonition for the God-fearing" shows that revelation profits only the heart prepared by taqwā: Scripture illumines the one who draws near in reverence.

mots.racine: ق ف و / ء ث ر
ص د ق
و ع ظ / و ق ي

versets.ar: وَلْيَحْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْإِنجِيلِ بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-yaḥkum ahlu l-injīli bimā anzala llāhu fīhi wa-man lam yaḥkum bimā anzala llāhu fa-ulāʾika humu l-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: Let the people of the Gospel judge by what God has sent down in it. Those who do not judge by what God has sent down — they are the disobedient.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the gradation of the three twin verses: whoever does not judge by what God has sent down is in turn a disbeliever, a wrongdoer, then a transgressor — each forsaking reveals a degree of estrangement. To judge by anything other than revelation is to set one's desire above the divine decree, and that is the mainspring of all transgression (fisq).

mots.racine: ح ك م
mots.sens: let the people of the Gospel judge

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.sens: by what God sent down in it

mots.racine: ف س ق

versets.ar: وَأَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَمُهَيْمِنًا عَلَيْهِ ۖ فَاحْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ عَمَّا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْحَقِّ ۚ لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مِنكُمْ شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهَاجًا ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً وَلَٰكِن لِّيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِي مَا آتَاكُمْ ۖ فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْرَاتِ ۚ إِلَى اللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-anzalnā ilayka l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayhi mina l-kitābi wa-muhayminan ʿalayhi fa-ḥkum baynahum bimā anzala llāhu wa-lā tattabiʿ ahwāʾahum ʿammā jāʾaka mina l-ḥaqqi li-kullin jaʿalnā minkum shirʿatan wa-minhājan wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-jaʿalakum ummatan wāḥidatan wa-lākin li-yabluwakum fī mā ātākum fa-stabiqū l-khayrāti ilā llāhi marjiʿukum jamīʿan fa-yunabbiʾukum bimā kuntum fīhi takhtalifūn
versets.trad: We have sent down to you the Book in truth, confirming the Scripture before it and standing as guardian over it. So judge between them by what God has sent down, and do not follow their desires away from the truth that has come to you. To each of you We have given a Law and a Way. Had God willed, He would have made you a single community, but He willed to test you in what He has given you. So vie with one another in good deeds. To God is the return of you all, and He will inform you of that wherein you differed.
versets.commentaire: "To each a Law and a Way": Qushayrī reads in this the wisdom of ordered plurality. God could have made men a single community; He willed the trial of diversity that hearts might vie in good. "Vie with one another in good deeds" then becomes the only contest worth running: the return is to Him, and He alone will untie that wherein you differ.

mots.racine: ه م ن
ش ر ع / ن ه ج
س ب ق / خ ي ر

versets.ar: وَأَنِ احْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ وَاحْذَرْهُمْ أَن يَفْتِنُوكَ عَن بَعْضِ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّمَا يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُصِيبَهُم بِبَعْضِ ذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ النَّاسِ لَفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ani ḥkum baynahum bimā anzala llāhu wa-lā tattabiʿ ahwāʾahum wa-ḥdharhum an yaftinūka ʿan baʿḍi mā anzala llāhu ilayka fa-in tawallaw fa-ʿlam annamā yurīdu llāhu an yuṣībahum bi-baʿḍi dhunūbihim wa-inna kathīran mina n-nāsi la-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: And judge between them by what God has sent down, and do not follow their desires, and beware of them lest they tempt you away from part of what God has sent down to you. If they turn away, know that God wills to afflict them for some of their sins. Many among mankind are indeed transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī warns the guide's heart against subtle temptation (fitna): the finest trial is not the open enemy, but the one who would turn you from a single particle of the truth received. To follow men's desires even in one point is to open the breach; fidelity must be entire, for the decree strikes those who turn away through some of their sins.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي
ف ت ن
ف س ق

versets.ar: أَفَحُكْمَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ يَبْغُونَ ۚ وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ اللَّهِ حُكْمًا لِّقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-ḥukma l-jāhiliyyati yabghūna wa-man aḥsanu mina llāhi ḥukman li-qawmin yūqinūn
versets.trad: Is it the judgement of the Age of Ignorance that they seek? And who is better than God in judgement for a people of certainty?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ties the best judgement to the heart's certainty (yaqīn): whoever sees with the eye of certainty knows that no judgement is fairer than God's, for He alone knows the depth of things. To prefer the judgement of the jāhiliyya is to return to the darkness of the ego; certainty rests only in God.

mots.racine: ح ك م / ج ه ل
ح س ن / ح ك م
mots.def: the supremacy of the divine verdict.

mots.racine: ي ق ن

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصَارَىٰ أَوْلِيَاءَ ۘ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم مِّنكُمْ فَإِنَّهُ مِنْهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū l-yahūda wa-n-naṣārā awliyāʾa baʿḍuhum awliyāʾu baʿḍin wa-man yatawallahum minkum fa-innahu minhum inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies; some of them are allies of others. Whoever among you allies with them is of them. God does not guide the wrongdoing people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises walāya (alliance, protection) to the level of the heart: to bind one's heart to whoever opposes God is to share his lot; the soul takes the colour of what it clings to. Exclusive fidelity is not blind hostility but the guarding of the heart: whoever places his support elsewhere than in God ranks among those he takes for refuge.

mots.racine: ء خ ذ / و ل ي
و ل ي
و ل ي
mots.sens: whoever allies with them is of them

versets.ar: فَتَرَى الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ يُسَارِعُونَ فِيهِمْ يَقُولُونَ نَخْشَىٰ أَن تُصِيبَنَا دَائِرَةٌ ۚ فَعَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَأْتِيَ بِالْفَتْحِ أَوْ أَمْرٍ مِّنْ عِندِهِ فَيُصْبِحُوا عَلَىٰ مَا أَسَرُّوا فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ نَادِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-tarā lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun yusāriʿūna fīhim yaqūlūna nakhshā an tuṣībanā dāʾiratun fa-ʿasā llāhu an yaʾtiya bi-l-fatḥi aw amrin min ʿindihi fa-yuṣbiḥū ʿalā mā asarrū fī anfusihim nādimīn
versets.trad: You see those in whose hearts is a sickness hastening toward them, saying: We fear a turn of fortune may strike us. Yet it may be that God will bring victory, or some command from Him, and then they will regret what they concealed within themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī diagnoses the "sickness of the heart" as doubt mixed with calculation: he whose heart is stricken seeks refuge with men out of fear of fortune, unaware that fortune is in God's hand alone. The final regret will lay bare the buried secret: what the heart conceals, God will bring to light.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / م ر ض
خ ش ي / د و ر
mots.sens: we fear a turn of fortune

mots.racine: ن د م

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَهَٰؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ ۙ إِنَّهُمْ لَمَعَكُمْ ۚ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا خَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlu lladhīna āmanū a-hāʾulāʾi lladhīna aqsamū bi-llāhi jahda aymānihim innahum la-maʿakum ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum fa-aṣbaḥū khāsirīn
versets.trad: The believers will say: Are these the ones who swore by God their most solemn oaths that they were with you? Their works have come to nothing, and they have become losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the work built on double-dealing collapses; solemn oaths avail nothing if the heart does not inhabit them. When the veil falls, the believers recognize the imposture, and the hypocrites' works prove vain — for what is not for God does not endure.

mots.racine: ق س م / ي م ن
ح ب ط
خ س ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَن يَرْتَدَّ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ يُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلَا يَخَافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لَائِمٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū man yartadda minkum ʿan dīnihi fa-sawfa yaʾtī llāhu bi-qawmin yuḥibbuhum wa-yuḥibbūnahu adhillatin ʿalā l-muʾminīna aʿizzatin ʿalā l-kāfirīna yujāhidūna fī sabīli llāhi wa-lā yakhāfūna lawmata lāʾimin dhālika faḍlu llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! Whoever among you turns away from his religion, God will bring a people whom He loves and who love Him, humble toward the believers, mighty against the disbelievers, striving in the path of God, and fearing no reproach of any reproacher. That is the bounty of God, which He gives to whom He wills. God is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing.
themes: mutual love between God and His chosen
versets.commentaire: Here is the luminous heart of the sūra. Qushayrī meditates the order of the words: He says "He loves them" before "they love Him" — His love for them precedes their love for Him; what you take to be your reaching toward Him is but the reflection of His choice that went before you. And he adds: to love God is to fear the reproach of none, for love fills the heart and leaves room in it for no other fear.

mots.racine: ر د د / د ي ن
ح ب ب
mots.sens: He loves them and they love Him

mots.racine: ذ ل ل / ع ز ز
mots.def: gentle toward believers, firm against disbelievers.

mots.racine: خ و ف / ل و م
mots.sens: fearing no reproach of any reproacher

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا وَلِيُّكُمُ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَهُمْ رَاكِعُونَ
versets.tr: innamā waliyyukumu llāhu wa-rasūluhu wa-lladhīna āmanū lladhīna yuqīmūna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-yuʾtūna z-zakāta wa-hum rākiʿūn
versets.trad: Your ally is none but God and His Messenger and those who believe — who establish the prayer, give the alms, and bow down humbly.
versets.commentaire: "Your ally is none but God": Qushayrī sees in this the restriction that liberates. To narrow walāya to God, His Messenger, and the believers is to wrest the heart from every other dependence. The humble bowing (rukūʿ) at the very heart of almsgiving signals that true alliance unites the body's service to the heart's humility.

mots.racine: و ل ي
ق و م / ص ل و
ر ك ع

versets.ar: وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْغَالِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man yatawalla llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-lladhīna āmanū fa-inna ḥizba llāhi humu l-ghālibūn
versets.trad: Whoever takes God and His Messenger and those who believe as allies — the party of God are the victorious.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the party of God is no faction among others, but those whose hearts are wholly turned to Him; their victory is first inward, for whoever has God as his support knows no defeat of the heart. The triumph (ghalaba) promised is the rule of the spirit over the soul, before any visible victory.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.sens: takes God and His Messenger as allies

mots.racine: ح ز ب
غ ل ب

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَكُمْ هُزُوًا وَلَعِبًا مِّنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَالْكُفَّارَ أَوْلِيَاءَ ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū lladhīna ttakhadhū dīnakum huzuwan wa-laʿiban mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba min qablikum wa-l-kuffāra awliyāʾa wa-ttaqū llāha in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not take as allies those who make a mockery and a game of your religion, from among those given the Book before you, nor the disbelievers. Fear God, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that to befriend those who turn religion to mockery is to consent to that mockery: friendship shares the table of the soul; one becomes like what one keeps company with. "Fear God if you are believers" ties faith to vigilance: the sincerity of belief is measured by the guard one keeps over one's attachments.

mots.racine: ه ز ء / ل ع ب
mots.sens: your religion as mockery and game

mots.racine: ك ف ر / و ل ي
ء م ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا نَادَيْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ اتَّخَذُوهَا هُزُوًا وَلَعِبًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā nādaytum ilā ṣ-ṣalāti ttakhadhūhā huzuwan wa-laʿiban dhālika bi-annahum qawmun lā yaʿqilūn
versets.trad: When you call to prayer, they take it as a mockery and a game. That is because they are a people who do not reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to mock the call to prayer betrays a heart bereft of intelligence (ʿaql) — not the intelligence of calculation, but the one that discerns the majesty of the divine summons. Whoever does not perceive the grandeur of the call to worship is blind to the very meaning of existence.

mots.racine: ن د و / ص ل و
ء خ ذ / ه ز ء
ع ق ل

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ هَلْ تَنقِمُونَ مِنَّا إِلَّا أَنْ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَمَا أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلُ وَأَنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi hal tanqimūna minnā illā an āmannā bi-llāhi wa-mā unzila ilaynā wa-mā unzila min qablu wa-anna aktharakum fāsiqūn
versets.trad: Say: O People of the Book! Do you resent us for anything but that we believe in God, in what has been sent down to us, and in what was sent down before, and because most of you are transgressors?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the paradox: they reproach the believers for faith itself — as though to believe in God and all His revelations were a crime. The Muslims' faith embraces what was revealed before; the People of the Book's grievance turns back upon them, revealing that it is their own deviation (fisq) that vexes them.

mots.racine: ن ق م
ء م ن
ن ز ل

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكَ مَثُوبَةً عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ مَن لَّعَنَهُ اللَّهُ وَغَضِبَ عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلَ مِنْهُمُ الْقِرَدَةَ وَالْخَنَازِيرَ وَعَبَدَ الطَّاغُوتَ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضَلُّ عَن سَوَاءِ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: qul hal unabbiʾukum bi-sharrin min dhālika mathūbatan ʿinda llāhi man laʿanahu llāhu wa-ghaḍiba ʿalayhi wa-jaʿala minhumu l-qiradata wa-l-khanāzīra wa-ʿabada ṭ-ṭāghūta ulāʾika sharrun makānan wa-aḍallu ʿan sawāʾi s-sabīl
versets.trad: Say: Shall I tell you of something worse than that as a recompense with God? Those whom God has cursed and with whom He is angry, of whom He made apes and swine, and who worshipped false gods — those are worse in station and further astray from the level path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the transformation into apes and swine as an inward parable: whoever worships his passion (ṭāghūt) is inwardly disfigured; the soul takes the form of what it venerates. The worst of stations is not the visible place but the straying far from the level path — the heart's estrangement from its Lord.

mots.racine: ش ر ر / ث و ب
ق ر د / خ ن ز ر
ع ب د / ط غ ي

versets.ar: وَإِذَا جَاءُوكُمْ قَالُوا آمَنَّا وَقَد دَّخَلُوا بِالْكُفْرِ وَهُمْ قَدْ خَرَجُوا بِهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā jāʾūkum qālū āmannā wa-qad dakhalū bi-l-kufri wa-hum qad kharajū bihi wa-llāhu aʿlamu bimā kānū yaktumūn
versets.trad: When they come to you they say, We believe, though they came in with disbelief and went out with it. God knows best what they were concealing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever enters with denial and leaves with it has merely passed through the faith of the lips; the heart remained at its threshold. "God knows what they conceal": no mask holds before the One who sees the secret of secrets, and the lips' profession changes nothing of the heart's state.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ء م ن
د خ ل / ك ف ر
ك ت م

versets.ar: وَتَرَىٰ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-tarā kathīran minhum yusāriʿūna fī l-ithmi wa-l-ʿudwāni wa-aklihimu s-suḥta la-biʾsa mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: You see many of them hastening into sin and transgression and the devouring of unlawful gain. How evil is what they do!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to hasten toward sin reveals a heart enslaved to passion; and to devour the unlawful (suḥt) extinguishes the inner light. The eagerness (musāraʿa) that should carry one toward good turns instead toward evil — a sign that the soul has usurped the heart's place in the governance of one's being.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ء ث م
ع د و
ء ك ل / س ح ت

versets.ar: لَوْلَا يَنْهَاهُمُ الرَّبَّانِيُّونَ وَالْأَحْبَارُ عَن قَوْلِهِمُ الْإِثْمَ وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: lawlā yanhāhumu r-rabbāniyyūna wa-l-aḥbāru ʿan qawlihimu l-ithma wa-aklihimu s-suḥta la-biʾsa mā kānū yaṣnaʿūn
versets.trad: Why do their masters and rabbis not forbid them from sinful speech and the devouring of unlawful gain? How evil is what they contrive!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the reproach upon the scholars who keep silent: whoever holds knowledge and forbids not the evil shares in the fault; the silence of the guide is a betrayal of guidance. The duty of the knower is to set straight; to renounce it out of complacency is to let the poison spread.

mots.racine: ن ه ي
mots.sens: why do they not forbid them

mots.racine: ق و ل / ء ث م
ص ن ع

versets.ar: وَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ يَدُ اللَّهِ مَغْلُولَةٌ ۚ غُلَّتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَلُعِنُوا بِمَا قَالُوا ۘ بَلْ يَدَاهُ مَبْسُوطَتَانِ يُنفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشَاءُ ۚ وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم مَّا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ طُغْيَانًا وَكُفْرًا ۚ وَأَلْقَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ ۚ كُلَّمَا أَوْقَدُوا نَارًا لِّلْحَرْبِ أَطْفَأَهَا اللَّهُ ۚ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَسَادًا ۚ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālati l-yahūdu yadu llāhi maghlūlatun ghullat aydīhim wa-luʿinū bimā qālū bal yadāhu mabsūṭatāni yunfiqu kayfa yashāʾu wa-la-yazīdanna kathīran minhum mā unzila ilayka min rabbika ṭughyānan wa-kufran wa-alqaynā baynahumu l-ʿadāwata wa-l-baghḍāʾa ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati kullamā awqadū nāran li-l-ḥarbi aṭfaʾahā llāhu wa-yasʿawna fī l-arḍi fasādan wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu l-mufsidīn
versets.trad: The Jews say: God's hand is fettered. It is their hands that are fettered, and they are cursed for what they say. Rather, His two hands are outstretched; He spends as He wills. What has been sent down to you from your Lord increases many of them in tyranny and disbelief. We have cast among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection. Whenever they kindle a fire for war, God extinguishes it. They strive for corruption in the land, but God does not love the corrupters.
versets.commentaire: "His two hands are outstretched": Qushayrī makes this image a summit of divine generosity (jūd). The Real pours out His gifts without measure or motive; whoever claims His hand is fettered projects upon Him his own avarice. And he observes that the same revelation which guides some increases others in rebellion: the light reveals hearts as much as it tries them.

mots.racine: ي د / غ ل ل
ي د / ب س ط

mots.ar: كُلَّمَا أَوْقَدُوا نَارًا لِّلْحَرْبِ أَطْفَأَهَا اللَّهُ
mots.tr: kullamā awqadū nāran li-l-ḥarbi aṭfaʾahā llāh
mots.racine: و ق د / ط ف ء
mots.sens: every war-fire they kindle, God puts out

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَكَفَّرْنَا عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَلَأَدْخَلْنَاهُمْ جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-law anna ahla l-kitābi āmanū wa-ttaqaw la-kaffarnā ʿanhum sayyiʾātihim wa-la-adkhalnāhum jannāti n-naʿīm
versets.trad: Had the People of the Book believed and feared God, We would have effaced their evil deeds and admitted them into Gardens of bliss.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī underscores the mercy of the offer: the door remains open — faith and fear would suffice to efface the faults and open the Gardens. God shuts no one within his past; the "had they" is a sigh of the grace that awaits the return, ready to turn evil deeds into favours.

mots.racine: ء م ن / و ق ي
ك ف ر / س و ء
mots.sens: We would efface their evil deeds

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن ع م

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ أَقَامُوا التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنجِيلَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِم مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ لَأَكَلُوا مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِم ۚ مِّنْهُمْ أُمَّةٌ مُّقْتَصِدَةٌ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ سَاءَ مَا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law annahum aqāmū t-tawrāta wa-l-injīla wa-mā unzila ilayhim min rabbihim la-akalū min fawqihim wa-min taḥti arjulihim minhum ummatun muqtaṣidatun wa-kathīrun minhum sāʾa mā yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Had they observed the Torah, the Gospel, and what was sent down to them from their Lord, they would have eaten from above them and from beneath their feet. Among them is a moderate community, but many of them — evil is what they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in "they would have eaten from above and beneath their feet" the promise of an abundance opened by obedience: when the heart stands upright, blessings descend from heaven and spring up from the earth. The "moderate" community (muqtaṣida) is the one that holds the middle way — faithful without excess, and in it the hope abides.

mots.racine: ق و م
ء ك ل / ف و ق
ء م م / ق ص د

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ مَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ وَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلْ فَمَا بَلَّغْتَ رِسَالَتَهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ النَّاسِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā r-rasūlu balligh mā unzila ilayka min rabbika wa-in lam tafʿal fa-mā ballaghta risālatahu wa-llāhu yaʿṣimuka mina n-nāsi inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: O Messenger! Proclaim what has been sent down to you from your Lord. If you do not, you will not have conveyed His message. God will protect you from men. God does not guide the disbelieving people.
versets.commentaire: "God will protect you from men": Qushayrī makes this guarantee the repose of the heart in its mission. Whom God protects has nothing to fear from the creature; the conveying of the truth admits neither softening nor concealment. To withhold part of the message would be not to have conveyed it — fidelity to the revealed trust is entire or it is not.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ن ز ل
mots.sens: proclaim what is sent to you

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ر س ل
mots.sens: you will not have conveyed His message

mots.racine: ع ص م

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لَسْتُمْ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ حَتَّىٰ تُقِيمُوا التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنجِيلَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۗ وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم مَّا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ طُغْيَانًا وَكُفْرًا ۖ فَلَا تَأْسَ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi lastum ʿalā shayʾin ḥattā tuqīmū t-tawrāta wa-l-injīla wa-mā unzila ilaykum min rabbikum wa-la-yazīdanna kathīran minhum mā unzila ilayka min rabbika ṭughyānan wa-kufran fa-lā taʾsa ʿalā l-qawmi l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Say: O People of the Book! You stand on nothing until you observe the Torah, the Gospel, and what has been sent down to you from your Lord. Yet what has been sent down to you from your Lord increases many of them in tyranny and disbelief. So do not grieve over the disbelieving people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to possess the Book without living it is to stand on nothing; knowledge that does not become practice is a burden, not a support. And he renews the consolation: "do not grieve" — grief for those who persist must not trouble the serenity of the one who conveys, for guidance belongs to God alone.

mots.racine: ك و ن / ش ي ء
ق و م
ء س و

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هَادُوا وَالصَّابِئُونَ وَالنَّصَارَىٰ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-lladhīna hādū wa-ṣ-ṣābiʾūna wa-n-naṣārā man āmana bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: Those who believe, the Jews, the Sabaeans, and the Christians — whoever believes in God and the Last Day and does righteousness — no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes "neither fear nor grief" the very definition of the heart's security (amn): whoever truly believes and acts uprightly is delivered from the fear of the future and the regret of the past; his dwelling is the instant in God. Whatever names are borne, it is sincere faith and good works that open onto this peace.

mots.racine: ص ب ء
ء م ن
mots.sens: believes in God and the Last Day

mots.racine: خ و ف

versets.ar: لَقَدْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ وَأَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ رُسُلًا ۖ كُلَّمَا جَاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ بِمَا لَا تَهْوَىٰ أَنفُسُهُمْ فَرِيقًا كَذَّبُوا وَفَرِيقًا يَقْتُلُونَ
versets.tr: laqad akhadhnā mīthāqa banī isrāʾīla wa-arsalnā ilayhim rusulan kullamā jāʾahum rasūlun bimā lā tahwā anfusuhum farīqan kadhdhabū wa-farīqan yaqtulūn
versets.trad: We took the covenant of the Children of Israel and sent to them messengers. Whenever a messenger came to them with what their souls did not desire, some they denied and some they killed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lays bare the spring of rejection: the soul's passion (hawā) cannot bear the truth that crosses it; it would sooner deny or kill the messenger than let itself be corrected. The whole history of prophetic refusal lies in this preference of the soul for its inclinations over guidance.

mots.racine: ء خ ذ / و ث ق
mots.sens: We took the covenant of the Children of Israel

mots.racine: ه و ي / ن ف س
mots.sens: what their souls did not desire

mots.racine: ف ر ق / ك ذ ب / ق ت ل
mots.sens: some they denied, some they killed

versets.ar: وَحَسِبُوا أَلَّا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ فَعَمُوا وَصَمُّوا ثُمَّ تَابَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ عَمُوا وَصَمُّوا كَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḥasibū allā takūna fitnatun fa-ʿamū wa-ṣammū thumma tāba llāhu ʿalayhim thumma ʿamū wa-ṣammū kathīrun minhum wa-llāhu baṣīrun bimā yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: They thought no trial would come, so they became blind and deaf. Then God turned to them, then many of them became blind and deaf again. God sees well what they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes blindness and deafness diseases of the inner organ: the heart's eye goes blind and the spirit's ear shuts when the soul believes itself safe from trial. The divine pardon is renewed, but so is the relapse — a sign that the cure endures only through a constant vigilance of the heart turned toward God.

mots.racine: ح س ب / ف ت ن
mots.sens: they thought no trial would come

mots.racine: ع م ي / ص م م
ت و ب

versets.ar: لَقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۖ وَقَالَ الْمَسِيحُ يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُ مَن يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ الْجَنَّةَ وَمَأْوَاهُ النَّارُ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ
versets.tr: laqad kafara lladhīna qālū inna llāha huwa l-masīḥu bnu maryama wa-qāla l-masīḥu yā banī isrāʾīla ʿbudū llāha rabbī wa-rabbakum innahu man yushrik bi-llāhi fa-qad ḥarrama llāhu ʿalayhi l-jannata wa-maʾwāhu n-nāru wa-mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min anṣār
versets.trad: They have disbelieved who say: God is the Messiah, son of Mary. Yet the Messiah said: O Children of Israel! Worship God, my Lord and your Lord. Whoever associates anything with God, God has forbidden him the Garden, and his abode is the Fire. The wrongdoers have no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the very word of ʿĪsā the refutation of those who deify him: "Worship God, my Lord and your Lord" — he places himself among the worshippers, not the worshipped. Association (shirk) is the one veil that mercy does not lift so long as it remains, for it denies the Oneness that is the ground of all nearness.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ر ب ب
mots.sens: worship God, my Lord and yours

mots.racine: ش ر ك
ح ر م / ج ن ن

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ كَفَرَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ ثَالِثُ ثَلَاثَةٍ ۘ وَمَا مِنْ إِلَٰهٍ إِلَّا إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۚ وَإِن لَّمْ يَنتَهُوا عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ لَيَمَسَّنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: laqad kafara lladhīna qālū inna llāha thālithu thalāthatin wa-mā min ilāhin illā ilāhun wāḥidun wa-in lam yantahū ʿammā yaqūlūna la-yamassanna lladhīna kafarū minhum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: They have disbelieved who say: God is the third of three. There is no god but One God. If they do not desist from what they say, a painful punishment will surely afflict those of them who disbelieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reaffirms the absolute Unity: "There is no god but One God" — all multiplicity in the divinity is a veil that sound reason and a pure heart reject. Oneness (tawḥīd) is no doctrine among others but the foundation of the whole way: to know the One is to be delivered from dispersion.

mots.racine: ث ل ث

mots.ar: مَا مِنْ إِلَٰهٍ إِلَّا إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ
mots.tr: mā min ilāhin illā ilāhun wāḥid
