ن ه ي

versets.ar: أَفَلَا يَتُوبُونَ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: a-fa-lā yatūbūna ilā llāhi wa-yastaghfirūnahu wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Will they not turn to God in repentance and seek His forgiveness? God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in this question a call full of tenderness: despite the gravity of their saying, the door of return stays wide open; the Forgiving, Compassionate One awaits their return. That God Himself, after the reproach, invites repentance shows that His mercy outweighs His wrath.

mots.racine: ت و ب
غ ف ر
غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: مَّا الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ وَأُمُّهُ صِدِّيقَةٌ ۖ كَانَا يَأْكُلَانِ الطَّعَامَ ۗ انظُرْ كَيْفَ نُبَيِّنُ لَهُمُ الْآيَاتِ ثُمَّ انظُرْ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: mā l-masīḥu bnu maryama illā rasūlun qad khalat min qablihi r-rusulu wa-ummuhu ṣiddīqatun kānā yaʾkulāni ṭ-ṭaʿāma nẓur kayfa nubayyinu lahumu l-āyāti thumma nẓur annā yuʾfakūn
versets.trad: The Messiah, son of Mary, was only a messenger, before whom messengers had passed; his mother was a woman of truth. Both of them ate food. See how We make the signs clear to them, then see how they are deluded.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the plain argument: one who eats and needs nourishment cannot be God; ʿĪsā and his mother shared the human condition, a sign that divinity is foreign to them. Mary is named "woman of truth" (ṣiddīqa) — a high degree of sainthood that yet does not efface creaturehood; and one marvels at the deluding before signs so clear.

mots.racine: ر س ل
ص د ق
mots.sens: his mother, a woman of truth

mots.racine: ء ك ل / ط ع م

versets.ar: قُلْ أَتَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا ۚ وَاللَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: qul a-taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi mā lā yamliku lakum ḍarran wa-lā nafʿan wa-llāhu huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: Say: Do you worship besides God what has no power to harm or benefit you? God is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to worship what holds neither harm nor good is to prostrate before impotence; worship is due to none but the One who hears all and knows all. The Names the All-Hearing (al-Samīʿ) and the All-Knowing (al-ʿAlīm) set against the deaf and ignorant idol the majesty of the Living who perceives even the secret of hearts.

mots.racine: ع ب د
م ل ك / ض ر ر / ن ف ع
س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لَا تَغْلُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا أَهْوَاءَ قَوْمٍ قَدْ ضَلُّوا مِن قَبْلُ وَأَضَلُّوا كَثِيرًا وَضَلُّوا عَن سَوَاءِ السَّبِيلِ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi lā taghlū fī dīnikum ghayra l-ḥaqqi wa-lā tattabiʿū ahwāʾa qawmin qad ḍallū min qablu wa-aḍallū kathīran wa-ḍallū ʿan sawāʾi s-sabīl
versets.trad: Say: O People of the Book! Do not exaggerate in your religion beyond the truth, and do not follow the desires of a people who went astray before, led many astray, and strayed from the level path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī warns against excess (ghuluww) in religion: to overstep the truth, even in the name of devotion, is to go astray; the level path holds the middle between negligence and exaggeration. To follow the passions of the misguided leads to a threefold straying: losing oneself, losing others, and deviating from the path of balance.

mots.racine: غ ل و
mots.sens: do not exaggerate in your religion

mots.racine: ح ق ق
ض ل ل / س ب ل

versets.ar: لُعِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ عَلَىٰ لِسَانِ دَاوُودَ وَعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا وَّكَانُوا يَعْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: luʿina lladhīna kafarū min banī isrāʾīla ʿalā lisāni dāwūda wa-ʿīsā bni maryama dhālika bimā ʿaṣaw wa-kānū yaʿtadūn
versets.trad: Those of the Children of Israel who disbelieved were cursed by the tongue of David and of Jesus, son of Mary, because they disobeyed and were transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī specifies the cause: the curse falls only upon persistent disobedience and transgression; it is the fruit of the act, not an arbitrary decree. That the reproach passes by the tongues of the prophets David and ʿĪsā shows that the rejection of truth ends by turning against one the very guides one ought to have followed.

mots.racine: ل ع ن / ك ف ر
ل س ن
ع ص ي / ع د و

versets.ar: كَانُوا لَا يَتَنَاهَوْنَ عَن مُّنكَرٍ فَعَلُوهُ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: kānū lā yatanāhawna ʿan munkarin faʿalūhu la-biʾsa mā kānū yafʿalūn
versets.trad: They did not forbid one another the wrong they did. How evil was what they used to do!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lays the blame upon passive complicity: to keep silent before the evil one sees committed is to share in it; the absence of mutual reproach corrupts the whole community. The duty to forbid the wrong (nahy ʿan al-munkar) is the salt that preserves the social body; to neglect it is to let the rot spread.

mots.racine: ن ه ي
ن ك ر / ف ع ل
ب ء س / ف ع ل

versets.ar: تَرَىٰ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا قَدَّمَتْ لَهُمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ أَن سَخِطَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَفِي الْعَذَابِ هُمْ خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: tarā kathīran minhum yatawallawna lladhīna kafarū la-biʾsa mā qaddamat lahum anfusuhum an sakhiṭa llāhu ʿalayhim wa-fī l-ʿadhābi hum khālidūn
versets.trad: You see many of them allying with the disbelievers. How evil is what their souls have sent ahead for them, that God is angry with them, and in the punishment they will abide.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what the soul sends ahead for itself when it allies with the enemies of God is but an evil that returns upon it; it prepares for itself wrath and lasting punishment. To bind oneself in affection to those who reject God is to let the soul lead the heart toward its own ruin.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ك ف ر
ق د م / ن ف س
س خ ط

versets.ar: وَلَوْ كَانُوا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالنَّبِيِّ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَا اتَّخَذُوهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ وَلَٰكِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law kānū yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-n-nabiyyi wa-mā unzila ilayhi mā ttakhadhūhum awliyāʾa wa-lākinna kathīran minhum fāsiqūn
versets.trad: Had they believed in God and the Prophet and what was sent down to him, they would not have taken them as allies. But many of them are transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the logical consequence of faith: true faith makes impossible the heart's alliance with those who reject God; where impious friendship persists, faith is absent or corrupt. The measure of belief's sincerity is thus the quality of the heart's attachments.

mots.racine: ء م ن
ء خ ذ / و ل ي
mots.sens: they would not have taken them as allies

mots.racine: ف س ق

versets.ar: لَتَجِدَنَّ أَشَدَّ النَّاسِ عَدَاوَةً لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا ۖ وَلَتَجِدَنَّ أَقْرَبَهُم مَّوَدَّةً لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّا نَصَارَىٰ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ مِنْهُمْ قِسِّيسِينَ وَرُهْبَانًا وَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: la-tajidanna ashadda n-nāsi ʿadāwatan li-lladhīna āmanū l-yahūda wa-lladhīna ashrakū wa-la-tajidanna aqrabahum mawaddatan li-lladhīna āmanū lladhīna qālū innā naṣārā dhālika bi-anna minhum qissīsīna wa-ruhbānan wa-annahum lā yastakbirūn
versets.trad: You will surely find the most hostile of men to the believers to be the Jews and the polytheists, and you will surely find the closest in affection to the believers those who say, We are Christians, because among them are priests and monks, and because they are not arrogant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī names the root of the heart's nearness: the absence of pride opens the door of affection; it is the humble — priests and monks given to recollection — who prove closest to the believers. Pride (istikbār) is the veil that separates; humility is the bridge that draws hearts toward the truth.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع د و
ق ر ب / و د د
ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا سَمِعُوا مَا أُنزِلَ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ تَرَىٰ أَعْيُنَهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ مِمَّا عَرَفُوا مِنَ الْحَقِّ ۖ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā samiʿū mā unzila ilā r-rasūli tarā aʿyunahum tafīḍu mina d-damʿi mimmā ʿarafū mina l-ḥaqqi yaqūlūna rabbanā āmannā fa-ktubnā maʿa sh-shāhidīn
versets.trad: When they hear what was sent down to the Messenger, you see their eyes overflow with tears at what they recognize of the truth. They say: Our Lord, we believe, so write us down among the witnesses.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes these tears the sign of an inward recognition: when the heart recognizes the truth, the eye overflows; the tear is the flowing of the soul that yields before the evidence of the Real. To ask to be written "among the witnesses" (shāhidīn) is the summit of avowal: not only to believe, but to attest what one has seen of the light.

mots.racine: ف ي ض / د م ع
ع ر ف / ح ق ق
mots.sens: at what they recognized of the truth

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ش ه د

versets.ar: وَمَا لَنَا لَا نُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَمَا جَاءَنَا مِنَ الْحَقِّ وَنَطْمَعُ أَن يُدْخِلَنَا رَبُّنَا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā lanā lā nuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-mā jāʾanā mina l-ḥaqqi wa-naṭmaʿu an yudkhilanā rabbunā maʿa l-qawmi ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: Why should we not believe in God and the truth that has come to us, while we long for our Lord to admit us among the righteous people?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī admires the spontaneous avowal: whoever has tasted the truth can no longer deny it; his faith becomes an ardent longing to be joined to the just. The hope (ṭamaʿ) of being admitted "among the righteous" is the lover's mainspring: he seeks not only salvation but the company of those whom God approves.

mots.racine: ء م ن
ط م ع / د خ ل
mots.sens: we long that He admit us

mots.racine: ص ل ح

versets.ar: فَأَثَابَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِمَا قَالُوا جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-athābahumu llāhu bimā qālū jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-dhālika jazāʾu l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: So God rewarded them, for what they said, with Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. That is the reward of those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes that the reward follows "what they said," words born of the heart's sincerity: the word that springs from a true recognition is worth a deed, and God inscribes it in the register of the beneficent. The eternal abode in the Gardens is the recompense of iḥsān — to worship God as if one saw Him.

mots.racine: ث و ب
خ ل د
ج ز ي / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jaḥīm
versets.trad: But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs — they are the companions of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets this verse against the one before it as shadow against light: to the tears of recognition answers the hardness of denial; to the bliss of the Gardens, the fire of separation. Destiny is decided in the heart's stance before the signs: to recognize and weep, or to deny and harden.

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ك ذ ب
mots.def: giving the lie to the verses.

mots.racine: ج ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُحَرِّمُوا طَيِّبَاتِ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَلَا تَعْتَدُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tuḥarrimū ṭayyibāti mā aḥalla llāhu lakum wa-lā taʿtadū inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-muʿtadīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not forbid the good things that God has made lawful to you, and do not transgress. God does not love the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī teaches the middle way: to forbid oneself what God has permitted is an excess as much as to transgress His limits; the level path is neither laxity nor forced austerity. To declare unlawful what God has made lawful is to impose upon one's soul a harshness the Law does not ask, and that is a subtle form of transgression.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ط ي ب
mots.sens: do not forbid the good things

mots.racine: ح ل ل
mots.sens: what God made lawful to you

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ع د و

versets.ar: وَكُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ حَلَالًا طَيِّبًا ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي أَنتُم بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kulū mimmā razaqakumu llāhu ḥalālan ṭayyiban wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī antum bihi muʾminūn
versets.trad: Eat of what God has provided you, lawful and good, and fear God, in whom you believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unites the enjoyment of the lawful with reverent fear: to eat the lawful with gratitude is not contrary to piety; on the contrary, it is to recognize the Giver in the gift. And he marks the tenderness of the close: "in whom you believe" — the fear of God is full only when mingled with love for the One whom one has recognized.

mots.racine: ء ك ل / ر ز ق
mots.sens: eat of what God provides you

mots.racine: ح ل ل / ط ي ب
ء م ن

versets.ar: لَا يُؤَاخِذُكُمُ اللَّهُ بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمَانِكُمْ وَلَٰكِن يُؤَاخِذُكُم بِمَا عَقَّدتُّمُ الْأَيْمَانَ ۖ فَكَفَّارَتُهُ إِطْعَامُ عَشَرَةِ مَسَاكِينَ مِنْ أَوْسَطِ مَا تُطْعِمُونَ أَهْلِيكُمْ أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ أَوْ تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ كَفَّارَةُ أَيْمَانِكُمْ إِذَا حَلَفْتُمْ ۚ وَاحْفَظُوا أَيْمَانَكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ آيَاتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: lā yuʾākhidhukumu llāhu bi-l-laghwi fī aymānikum wa-lākin yuʾākhidhukum bimā ʿaqqadtumu l-aymāna fa-kaffāratuhu iṭʿāmu ʿasharati masākīna min awsaṭi mā tuṭʿimūna ahlīkum aw kiswatuhum aw taḥrīru raqabatin fa-man lam yajid fa-ṣiyāmu thalāthati ayyāmin dhālika kaffāratu aymānikum idhā ḥalaftum wa-ḥfaẓū aymānakum ka-dhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakum āyātihi laʿallakum tashkurūn
versets.trad: God does not take you to task for thoughtless oaths, but He takes you to task for the oaths you have deliberately bound. Its expiation is the feeding of ten poor people from the average of what you feed your families, or clothing them, or freeing a slave; and whoever cannot find the means, let him fast three days. That is the expiation of your oaths when you swear. Keep your oaths. Thus God makes His signs clear to you, that you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: "Keep your oaths": Qushayrī makes the guarding of one's given word a pillar of faithfulness. The oath is a knot with God as witness; to break it without expiation is to betray that witnessing. That God provides a graded expiation (to feed, clothe, free, or fast) manifests His gentleness: He always opens a way of ransom to one who has failed.

mots.racine: ل غ و / ي م ن
ك ف ر / ط ع م
ح ر ر / ر ق ب
ح ف ظ

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالْأَنصَابُ وَالْأَزْلَامُ رِجْسٌ مِّنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū innamā l-khamru wa-l-maysiru wa-l-anṣābu wa-l-azlāmu rijsun min ʿamali sh-shayṭāni fa-jtanibūhu laʿallakum tufliḥūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Wine, gambling, idol-altars, and divining arrows are an abomination, the work of Satan. So avoid it, that you may prosper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in these prohibitions so many veils that Satan stretches before the heart: wine clouds lucidity, gambling enslaves to greed; whatever turns one from the remembrance of God is the work of the tempter. "Avoid it" (ijtinâb) means to withdraw resolutely, for prosperity (falāḥ) comes only to the heart freed of what intoxicates and disperses it.

mots.racine: خ م ر / ي س ر
ر ج س / ع م ل
ج ن ب

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَن يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَدَاوَةَ وَالْبَغْضَاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلَاةِ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّنتَهُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yurīdu sh-shayṭānu an yūqiʿa baynakumu l-ʿadāwata wa-l-baghḍāʾa fī l-khamri wa-l-maysiri wa-yaṣuddakum ʿan dhikri llāhi wa-ʿani ṣ-ṣalāti fa-hal antum muntahūn
versets.trad: Satan only wishes to sow enmity and hatred among you through wine and gambling, and to bar you from the remembrance of God and from prayer. Will you not then desist?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the enemy's strategy: Satan's aim is not wine for its own sake, but what it provokes — discord among hearts and forgetfulness of God. The worst effect of intoxication and gambling is to bar one from dhikr and prayer, that is, to cut the living bond with the Lord. "Will you not then desist?" is a pressing exhortation.

mots.racine: و ق ع / ع د و
ص د د / ذ ك ر
ن ه ي

versets.ar: وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَاحْذَرُوا ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا عَلَىٰ رَسُولِنَا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-aṭīʿū r-rasūla wa-ḥdharū fa-in tawallaytum fa-ʿlamū annamā ʿalā rasūlinā l-balāghu l-mubīn
versets.trad: Obey God and obey the Messenger, and beware. If you turn away, know that the duty of Our Messenger is only the clear conveyance.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: obedience to the Messenger is obedience to God made visible; whoever separates the two has obeyed neither. And the Messenger's responsibility stops at clear conveyance (balāgh mubīn): he guides to the door, but it is God who opens hearts; the messenger is not answerable for the refusal.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر س ل
ح ذ ر
ب ل غ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: لَيْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوا إِذَا مَا اتَّقَوا وَّآمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ ثُمَّ اتَّقَوا وَّآمَنُوا ثُمَّ اتَّقَوا وَّأَحْسَنُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: laysa ʿalā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti junāḥun fīmā ṭaʿimū idhā mā ttaqaw wa-āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti thumma ttaqaw wa-āmanū thumma ttaqaw wa-aḥsanū wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: There is no blame upon those who believe and do righteous deeds for what they have eaten, so long as they fear God, believe, and do righteous deeds, then fear God and believe, then fear God and do good. God loves those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the threefold repetition "they fear... they fear... they fear" an ascent of stations: the first fear turns away from evil, the second confirms faith, the third raises to excellence (iḥsān). The summit is iḥsān, which seals the gradation and calls down the divine love: "God loves the beneficent" — to worship as though one saw Him.

mots.racine: ج ن ح / ط ع م
mots.sens: no blame for what they ate

mots.racine: و ق ي / ء م ن

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ اللَّهُ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الصَّيْدِ تَنَالُهُ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِمَاحُكُمْ لِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ مَن يَخَافُهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ۚ فَمَنِ اعْتَدَىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَهُ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū la-yabluwannakumu llāhu bi-shayʾin mina ṣ-ṣaydi tanāluhu aydīkum wa-rimāḥukum li-yaʿlama llāhu man yakhāfuhu bi-l-ghaybi fa-mani ʿtadā baʿda dhālika fa-lahu ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! God will surely test you with some of the game that your hands and your spears can reach, so that God may know who fears Him in the unseen. Whoever transgresses after that, for him is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: "Who fears Him in the unseen (bi-l-ghayb)": Qushayrī makes this invisible fear the criterion of sincerity. The trial of game within reach reveals who restrains his hand out of fear of God where none can see. True piety is not what one displays, but what keeps watch in secret, when only the divine presence is witness.

mots.racine: ب ل و
ن ي ل / ي د / ر م ح
mots.sens: your hands and spears can reach

mots.racine: خ و ف / غ ي ب
mots.sens: who fears Him in the unseen

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقْتُلُوا الصَّيْدَ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۚ وَمَن قَتَلَهُ مِنكُم مُّتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاءٌ مِّثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ هَدْيًا بَالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَٰلِكَ صِيَامًا لِّيَذُوقَ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِ ۗ عَفَا اللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ ۚ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انتِقَامٍ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā taqtulū ṣ-ṣayda wa-antum ḥurumun wa-man qatalahu minkum mutaʿammidan fa-jazāʾun mithlu mā qatala mina n-naʿami yaḥkumu bihi dhawā ʿadlin minkum hadyan bāligha l-kaʿbati aw kaffāratun ṭaʿāmu masākīna aw ʿadlu dhālika ṣiyāman li-yadhūqa wabāla amrihi ʿafā llāhu ʿammā salafa wa-man ʿāda fa-yantaqimu llāhu minhu wa-llāhu ʿazīzun dhū ntiqām
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not kill game while you are in the state of consecration. Whoever among you kills it deliberately, the compensation is the like of what he killed, of livestock, as judged by two just men among you, an offering brought to the Kaaba; or as an expiation, the feeding of the poor, or the equivalent in fasting, that he may taste the burden of his deed. God has pardoned what is past, but whoever returns to it, God will take vengeance on him. God is Mighty, Lord of vengeance.
themes: tasting the weight of the deed
versets.commentaire: "That he may taste the burden of his deed": Qushayrī makes the expiation a turning back upon oneself. The compensation is not mere punishment but pedagogy — it makes the heart feel the weight of the transgression so that it may turn away. That God pardons "what is past" and threatens only the relapse reveals the patience of His mercy, which always leaves a chance for repentance.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ص ي د / ح ر م
mots.sens: do not kill game in consecration

mots.racine: ج ز ي / م ث ل
ذ و ق / و ب ل
mots.sens: to taste the burden of his deed

versets.ar: أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ مَتَاعًا لَّكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ ۖ وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ مَا دُمْتُمْ حُرُمًا ۗ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: uḥilla lakum ṣaydu l-baḥri wa-ṭaʿāmuhu matāʿan lakum wa-li-s-sayyārati wa-ḥurrima ʿalaykum ṣaydu l-barri mā dumtum ḥuruman wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī ilayhi tuḥsharūn
versets.trad: Lawful to you is the catch of the sea and its food, a provision for you and for travellers; but the game of the land is forbidden to you so long as you are in the state of consecration. Fear God, to whom you will be gathered.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the prescription with the reminder of the return: fear the One to whom you will be gathered — every permission and prohibition takes its meaning in the prospect of the final meeting. The gift of the sea offered to the traveller signals God's gentleness toward the weak; yet the consecration remains a discipline that recalls the transcendence of the Beloved.

mots.sens: the catch of the sea and its food

versets.ar: جَعَلَ اللَّهُ الْكَعْبَةَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ قِيَامًا لِّلنَّاسِ وَالشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ وَالْهَدْيَ وَالْقَلَائِدَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: jaʿala llāhu l-kaʿbata l-bayta l-ḥarāma qiyāman li-n-nāsi wa-sh-shahra l-ḥarāma wa-l-hadya wa-l-qalāʾida dhālika li-taʿlamū anna llāha yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-anna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīm
versets.trad: God has made the Kaaba, the Sacred House, a standing support for mankind, and the sacred month, the offering, and the garlands, that you may know that God knows what is in the heavens and on the earth, and that God knows all things.
versets.commentaire: "A standing support for mankind" (qiyāman li-n-nās): Qushayrī makes the Kaaba the centre toward which hearts straighten themselves. The Sacred House is a visible pole of the invisible, where bodies orient themselves as souls do toward their Lord. The instituted rites are so many signs by which the servant learns that God knows all that the heavens and the earth contain.

versets.ar: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: iʿlamū anna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqābi wa-anna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Know that God is severe in punishment and that God is Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates the joining of the two sides: He joins the severity of punishment to the clemency of pardon, that the believer's heart may dwell between fear and hope, never slumbering nor despairing. To know these two Names together is to hold the balance of the way: neither presumption nor despair.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
غ ف ر / ر ح م
ع ل م

versets.ar: مَّا عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا تَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: mā ʿalā r-rasūli illā l-balāghu wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā tubdūna wa-mā taktumūn
versets.trad: The duty of the Messenger is only conveyance. God knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Messenger conveys; God alone knows the manifest and the hidden and judges hearts. The guide's responsibility stops at conveyance; what unfolds in the secret of souls belongs to the knowledge of the One who sees the apparent (mā tubdūn) as well as the concealed (mā taktumūn).

mots.racine: ر س ل / ب ل غ
ب د و
ك ت م

versets.ar: قُل لَّا يَسْتَوِي الْخَبِيثُ وَالطَّيِّبُ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ كَثْرَةُ الْخَبِيثِ ۚ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ يَا أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: qul lā yastawī l-khabīthu wa-ṭ-ṭayyibu wa-law aʿjabaka kathratu l-khabīthi fa-ttaqū llāha yā ulī l-albābi laʿallakum tufliḥūn
versets.trad: Say: the bad and the good are not equal, even though the abundance of the bad may please you. So fear God, O people of understanding, that you may prosper.
themes: the call to people of understanding
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the bad does not equal the good, however abundant; quantity does not change nature. The soul's eye is seduced by the great number of the bad (kathratu l-khabīth), but the heart's eye, lit by taqwā, discerns that the rare and pure is worth more than the abundant and defiled. Hence the call is addressed "to people of understanding" (ulī l-albāb), to those whose discernment reaches the depth of things.

mots.racine: س و ي / خ ب ث / ط ي ب
mots.sens: the bad and the good are not equal

mots.racine: ك ث ر / خ ب ث
mots.def: the great number of the vile.

mots.racine: ل ب ب

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَسْأَلُوا عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ إِن تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ وَإِن تَسْأَلُوا عَنْهَا حِينَ يُنَزَّلُ الْقُرْآنُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْهَا ۗ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tasʾalū ʿan ashyāʾa in tubda lakum tasuʾkum wa-in tasʾalū ʿanhā ḥīna yunazzalu l-qurʾānu tubda lakum ʿafā llāhu ʿanhā wa-llāhu ghafūrun ḥalīm
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not ask about things which, if shown to you, would distress you. If you ask about them while the Qur'an is being sent down, they will be made clear to you. God has pardoned that; God is Forgiving, Forbearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this counsel a school of adab: indiscreet curiosity about what God has veiled is a lack of courtesy toward the Veiler; better to welcome what is given than to demand what is withheld. God, the Forbearing (al-Ḥalīm), has passed certain things over in silence out of mercy — to probe them is to lift a veil that wisdom had set.

mots.racine: س ء ل
ب د و / س و ء
mots.sens: if shown, they would distress you

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ح ل م

versets.ar: قَدْ سَأَلَهَا قَوْمٌ مِّن قَبْلِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا بِهَا كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: qad saʾalahā qawmun min qablikum thumma aṣbaḥū bihā kāfirīn
versets.trad: A people before you asked such questions, then became disbelievers because of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever demands the unveiling before he is ready risks stumbling over what he has gained; the question born of curiosity, not of need, can lead to denial. The example of the ancients warns: knowledge that does not descend into a prepared heart becomes a stumbling-block rather than a light.

mots.racine: س ء ل
mots.sens: a people before you asked them

mots.racine: ك ف ر

versets.ar: مَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ مِن بَحِيرَةٍ وَلَا سَائِبَةٍ وَلَا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلَا حَامٍ ۙ وَلَٰكِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ ۖ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: mā jaʿala llāhu min baḥīratin wa-lā sāʾibatin wa-lā waṣīlatin wa-lā ḥāmin wa-lākinna lladhīna kafarū yaftarūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba wa-aktharuhum lā yaʿqilūn
versets.trad: God did not institute the Baḥīra, nor the Sāʾiba, nor the Waṣīla, nor the Ḥāmī; but those who disbelieve forge lies against God, and most of them do not reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to forge prohibitions God has not prescribed is to invent a religion to one's own fancy; the lie against God is born of a reason (ʿaql) that has abdicated. These beasts consecrated by pre-Islamic superstition are the image of every prohibition or rite a man invents for himself, substituting his imagination for the revealed decree.

mots.racine: ب ح ر / س ي ب
ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
ع ق ل

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا إِلَىٰ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ وَإِلَى الرَّسُولِ قَالُوا حَسْبُنَا مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا ۚ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ آبَاؤُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum taʿālaw ilā mā anzala llāhu wa-ilā r-rasūli qālū ḥasbunā mā wajadnā ʿalayhi ābāʾanā a-wa-law kāna ābāʾuhum lā yaʿlamūna shayʾan wa-lā yahtadūn
versets.trad: When it is said to them, Come to what God has sent down and to the Messenger, they say: Sufficient for us is what we found our fathers upon. Even though their fathers knew nothing and were not guided?
themes: the blind imitation of the fathers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī denounces servile attachment to custom: to be content with what one found among the fathers, without scrutiny, is to prefer habit to truth. The call "come to what God has sent down" invites one to leave the comfort of imitation (taqlīd) for the light of guidance; to follow misguided forefathers is to inherit their straying.

mots.racine: ع ل و / ن ز ل
mots.sens: come to what God sent down

mots.racine: ح س ب / و ج د
mots.sens: what our fathers did suffices us

mots.racine: ع ل م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلَيْكُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۖ لَا يَضُرُّكُم مَّن ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ ۚ إِلَى اللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ʿalaykum anfusakum lā yaḍurrukum man ḍalla idhā htadaytum ilā llāhi marjiʿukum jamīʿan fa-yunabbiʾukum bimā kuntum taʿmalūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Take care of your own souls. Whoever goes astray cannot harm you if you are rightly guided. To God is the return of you all, and He will inform you of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: "Take care of your own souls": Qushayrī makes this verse a call to inward vigilance. Watch over your soul before judging another's; whoever stands rightly guided is not harmed by the straying of others. This is no call to indifference — the duty to enjoin good remains — but a reminder that salvation begins with the guarding of one's own heart.

mots.racine: ن ف س

mots.ar: لَا يَضُرُّكُم مَّن ضَلَّ إِذَا اهْتَدَيْتُمْ
mots.tr: lā yaḍurrukum man ḍalla idhā htadaytum
mots.racine: ض ر ر / ض ل ل / ه د ي
mots.sens: the strayer does not harm you if guided

mots.racine: ر ج ع

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ حِينَ الْوَصِيَّةِ اثْنَانِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ إِنْ أَنتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَأَصَابَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۚ تَحْبِسُونَهُمَا مِن بَعْدِ الصَّلَاةِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ لَا نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ثَمَنًا وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰ ۙ وَلَا نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا إِذًا لَّمِنَ الْآثِمِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū shahādatu baynikum idhā ḥaḍara aḥadakumu l-mawtu ḥīna l-waṣiyyati thnāni dhawā ʿadlin minkum aw ākharāni min ghayrikum in antum ḍarabtum fī l-arḍi fa-aṣābatkum muṣībatu l-mawti taḥbisūnahumā min baʿdi ṣ-ṣalāti fa-yuqsimāni bi-llāhi ini rtabtum lā nashtarī bihi thamanan wa-law kāna dhā qurbā wa-lā naktumu shahādata llāhi innā idhan la-mina l-āthimīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Let testimony among you, when death approaches one of you, at the time of bequest, be by two just men among you, or two others from outside, if you are travelling in the land and the calamity of death befalls you. You shall detain them after the prayer, and they shall swear by God, if you doubt: We will not sell it for any price, even for a kinsman, nor will we conceal the testimony of God, for then we should be among the sinners.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the witnesses' oath a school of ultimate sincerity: not to sell the testimony of God at any price, even for a kinsman, is to hold the truth dearer than any tie. That the counsel surrounds the hour of death underlines that fidelity must abide to the last breath: whoever conceals the testimony of God ranks among the sinners.

mots.racine: ش ه د
ع د ل
ش ر ي / ث م ن
mots.sens: we will not sell it for any price

mots.racine: ك ت م / ش ه د
mots.sens: we will not conceal God's testimony

versets.ar: فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَىٰ أَنَّهُمَا اسْتَحَقَّا إِثْمًا فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَوْلَيَانِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ لَشَهَادَتُنَا أَحَقُّ مِن شَهَادَتِهِمَا وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنَا إِنَّا إِذًا لَّمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in ʿuthira ʿalā annahumā staḥaqqā ithman fa-ākharāni yaqūmāni maqāmahumā mina lladhīna staḥaqqa ʿalayhimu l-awlayāni fa-yuqsimāni bi-llāhi la-shahādatunā aḥaqqu min shahādatihimā wa-mā ʿtadaynā innā idhan la-mina ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: But if it is found that the two have committed perjury, two others shall stand in their place, from among those wronged by them, and they shall swear by God: Our testimony is truer than theirs, and we have not transgressed; for then we should be among the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: truth always ends by re-establishing itself; if testimony is falsified, God raises up those who will restore the right. That the oath invokes God's justice ("for then we should be wrongdoers") shows that testimony is no formality but a pledge before the supreme Witness — who sees sincerity as He sees the lie.

mots.racine: ع ث ر / ح ق ق
ق و م
ش ه د / ح ق ق

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰ أَن يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهَا أَوْ يَخَافُوا أَن تُرَدَّ أَيْمَانٌ بَعْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ ۗ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاسْمَعُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: dhālika adnā an yaʾtū bi-sh-shahādati ʿalā wajhihā aw yakhāfū an turadda aymānun baʿda aymānihim wa-ttaqū llāha wa-smaʿū wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: That is more likely to make them give true testimony, or to fear that oaths may be refuted after their oaths. Fear God and listen: God does not guide the disobedient people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī closes the discussion of testimony with a call to a twofold disposition: fear God and listen — fear keeps the heart upright, listening makes it open to guidance. The system of oaths aims to bring one nearer the true (adnā); but no procedure supplies for taqwā: God does not guide one who has turned away in disobedience.

mots.ar: أَدْنَىٰ أَن يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهَا
mots.tr: adnā an yaʾtū bi-sh-shahādati ʿalā wajhihā
mots.racine: د ن و / ش ه د
و ق ي / س م ع
ف س ق

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ اللَّهُ الرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَا أُجِبْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا لَا عِلْمَ لَنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
versets.tr: yawma yajmaʿu llāhu r-rusula fa-yaqūlu mādhā ujibtum qālū lā ʿilma lanā innaka anta ʿallāmu l-ghuyūb
versets.trad: On the Day when God gathers the messengers and says: What response were you given? They will say: We have no knowledge. You alone are the Knower of all that is hidden.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates the prophets' avowal: "We have no knowledge" — at the threshold of the Presence, even the messengers commit all knowledge to the One who knows the unseen. This effacement is not ignorance but supreme courtesy: before the Knower of all that is hidden (ʿallām al-ghuyūb), the creature's knowledge knows itself to be veil and nothingness.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ر س ل
mots.def: the summons of the Last Day.

mots.racine: ع ل م
ع ل م / غ ي ب
mots.sens: the Knower of all hidden things

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ اللَّهُ يَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ اذْكُرْ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَتِكَ إِذْ أَيَّدتُّكَ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ تُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلًا ۖ وَإِذْ عَلَّمْتُكَ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَالتَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنجِيلَ ۖ وَإِذْ تَخْلُقُ مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ بِإِذْنِي فَتَنفُخُ فِيهَا فَتَكُونُ طَيْرًا بِإِذْنِي ۖ وَتُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ بِإِذْنِي ۖ وَإِذْ تُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِي ۖ وَإِذْ كَفَفْتُ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ عَنكَ إِذْ جِئْتَهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: idh qāla llāhu yā ʿīsā bna maryama dhkur niʿmatī ʿalayka wa-ʿalā wālidatika idh ayyadtuka bi-rūḥi l-qudusi tukallimu n-nāsa fī l-mahdi wa-kahlan wa-idh ʿallamtuka l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-t-tawrāta wa-l-injīla wa-idh takhluqu mina ṭ-ṭīni ka-hayʾati ṭ-ṭayri bi-idhnī fa-tanfukhu fīhā fa-takūnu ṭayran bi-idhnī wa-tubriʾu l-akmaha wa-l-abraṣa bi-idhnī wa-idh tukhriju l-mawtā bi-idhnī wa-idh kafaftu banī isrāʾīla ʿanka idh jiʾtahum bi-l-bayyināti fa-qāla lladhīna kafarū minhum in hādhā illā siḥrun mubīn
versets.trad: When God said: O Jesus, son of Mary! Remember My favour upon you and upon your mother, when I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit, so that you spoke to people in the cradle and in maturity; and when I taught you the Book and wisdom, the Torah and the Gospel; and when you fashioned from clay the likeness of a bird by My leave, and breathed into it, and it became a bird by My leave; and you healed the blind from birth and the leper by My leave; and when you brought forth the dead by My leave; and when I held back the Children of Israel from you when you came to them with clear proofs, and those of them who disbelieved said: This is nothing but manifest sorcery.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the refrain "by My leave" (bi-idhnī), repeated at every wonder: the miracles of ʿĪsā are not his but Mine; he is the place of manifestation, not the source of power. To give life to clay, to heal, to raise the dead — always "by My leave": no power acts save through the Real, and the most favoured servant remains a pure vessel of grace.

mots.racine: ء ي د / ق د س
mots.sens: I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit

mots.racine: ء ذ ن
خ ر ج / م و ت
س ح ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوَارِيِّينَ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي قَالُوا آمَنَّا وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh awḥaytu ilā l-ḥawāriyyīna an āminū bī wa-bi-rasūlī qālū āmannā wa-shhad bi-annanā muslimūn
versets.trad: And when I revealed to the apostles: Believe in Me and in My Messenger. They said: We believe; bear witness that we are submitted (muslimūn).
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the apostles are not content to believe; they ask that their submission be attested — for the faith of the heart longs to be sealed by witnessing. To declare oneself "submitted" (muslimūn) is the fulfilment of islām in its first sense: the total surrender of the heart to the will of the Beloved.

mots.racine: و ح ي / ح و ر
ء م ن
س ل م

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ الْحَوَارِيُّونَ يَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ أَن يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا مَائِدَةً مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ ۖ قَالَ اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: idh qāla l-ḥawāriyyūna yā ʿīsā bna maryama hal yastaṭīʿu rabbuka an yunazzila ʿalaynā māʾidatan mina s-samāʾi qāla ttaqū llāha in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: When the apostles said: O Jesus, son of Mary! Can your Lord send down to us a table from heaven? He said: Fear God, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the subtle slip of the request: "Can your Lord?" betrays a remnant of doubt disguised as a plea. ʿĪsā's answer — "fear God, if you are believers" — sets the intention straight: true faith does not put God to the test but commits itself to Him; to ask for a sign in order to believe is still not to believe.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر ب ب
م ي د / س م و
و ق ي / ء م ن
mots.sens: fear God if you are believers

versets.ar: قَالُوا نُرِيدُ أَن نَّأْكُلَ مِنْهَا وَتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُنَا وَنَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ صَدَقْتَنَا وَنَكُونَ عَلَيْهَا مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: qālū nurīdu an naʾkula minhā wa-taṭmaʾinna qulūbunā wa-naʿlama an qad ṣadaqtanā wa-nakūna ʿalayhā mina sh-shāhidīn
versets.trad: They said: We wish to eat of it, that our hearts may be at peace, that we may know you have told us the truth, and that we may be among the witnesses of it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ennobles the request by reading in it the quest for serenity (iṭmiʾnān): the heart seeks repose in certainty, and the sight of the sign confirms its nascent faith. Like Abraham asking to see the resurrection "that my heart may be at peace," the apostles doubt not from unbelief but long to pass from heard faith to seen faith, that they may attest it.

mots.racine: ء ك ل
ط م ن / ق ل ب

versets.ar: قَالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا أَنزِلْ عَلَيْنَا مَائِدَةً مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ تَكُونُ لَنَا عِيدًا لِّأَوَّلِنَا وَآخِرِنَا وَآيَةً مِّنكَ ۖ وَارْزُقْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla ʿīsā bnu maryama llāhumma rabbanā anzil ʿalaynā māʾidatan mina s-samāʾi takūnu lanā ʿīdan li-awwalinā wa-ākhirinā wa-āyatan minka wa-rzuqnā wa-anta khayru r-rāziqīn
versets.trad: Jesus, son of Mary, said: O God, our Lord! Send down to us a table from heaven, to be a festival for us, for the first of us and the last, and a sign from You. And provide for us, for You are the best of providers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī savours the delicacy of the invocation: ʿĪsā asks not only for the food but that it be "a sign from You" — he turns the gift toward the Giver. "You are the best of providers": the true provider is never the visible means but the One who sends down sustenance from the heavens; to recognize this is to eat in the presence of the Giver.

mots.racine: ء ل ه / ر ب ب
ع و د
mots.sens: to be a festival for us

mots.racine: ر ز ق
mots.sens: You are the best of providers

versets.ar: قَالَ اللَّهُ إِنِّي مُنَزِّلُهَا عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بَعْدُ مِنكُمْ فَإِنِّي أُعَذِّبُهُ عَذَابًا لَّا أُعَذِّبُهُ أَحَدًا مِّنَ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla llāhu innī munazziluhā ʿalaykum fa-man yakfur baʿdu minkum fa-innī uʿadhdhibuhu ʿadhāban lā uʿadhdhibuhu aḥadan mina l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: God said: I will send it down to you. But whoever among you disbelieves after that, I shall punish him with a punishment with which I have punished no one in all the worlds.
themes: the gravity of ingratitude after the sign
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the more manifest the gift, the graver the ingratitude that follows it; to disbelieve after seeing the sign sent down from heaven has no excuse. The promise of an unparalleled punishment measures the measure of the grace: from whom much is shown, much is required; the veil after the unveiling is the worst of blindnesses.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.sens: I will send it down to you

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ع ذ ب
mots.sens: a punishment inflicted on none other

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ اللَّهُ يَا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ أَأَنتَ قُلْتَ لِلنَّاسِ اتَّخِذُونِي وَأُمِّيَ إِلَٰهَيْنِ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ سُبْحَانَكَ مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أَقُولَ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِحَقٍّ ۚ إِن كُنتُ قُلْتُهُ فَقَدْ عَلِمْتَهُ ۚ تَعْلَمُ مَا فِي نَفْسِي وَلَا أَعْلَمُ مَا فِي نَفْسِكَ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla llāhu yā ʿīsā bna maryama a-anta qulta li-n-nāsi ttakhidhūnī wa-ummiya ilāhayni min dūni llāhi qāla subḥānaka mā yakūnu lī an aqūla mā laysa lī bi-ḥaqqin in kuntu qultuhu fa-qad ʿalimtahu taʿlamu mā fī nafsī wa-lā aʿlamu mā fī nafsika innaka anta ʿallāmu l-ghuyūb
versets.trad: And when God said: O Jesus, son of Mary! Did you say to people, Take me and my mother as two gods besides God? He said: Glory be to You! It is not for me to say what I have no right to say. Had I said it, You would have known it. You know what is in my soul, but I do not know what is in Yours. You alone are the Knower of all that is hidden.
themes: the divine knowledge of the secret
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marvels at the infinite courtesy of the reply: "You know what is in my soul, and I do not know what is in Yours" — here is the limit the servant never crosses: God penetrates the secret of the soul, but none penetrates the secret of God. This avowal is the summit of adab: ʿĪsā confesses his creaturehood in the very asymmetry of knowledge.

ع ل م / ن ف س
mots.sens: You know what is in my soul

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.sens: and I know not what is in Yours

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب

versets.ar: مَا قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا مَا أَمَرْتَنِي بِهِ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ ۚ وَكُنتُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا مَّا دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَنِي كُنتَ أَنتَ الرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنتَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: mā qultu lahum illā mā amartanī bihi ani ʿbudū llāha rabbī wa-rabbakum wa-kuntu ʿalayhim shahīdan mā dumtu fīhim fa-lammā tawaffaytanī kunta anta r-raqība ʿalayhim wa-anta ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīd
versets.trad: I said to them only what You commanded me: Worship God, my Lord and your Lord. I was a witness over them as long as I remained among them; but when You took me to Yourself, You were the Watcher over them, and You are Witness over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī savours the servant's withdrawal behind his Lord: "when You took me, You became their Watcher" — the witness withdraws to make room for the eternal Witness. ʿĪsā conveyed only the command received; his watching stops with his presence, but God's has neither beginning nor end: He is the Witness over all things, forever.

mots.racine: ء م ر
و ف ي
ر ق ب

versets.ar: إِن تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبَادُكَ ۖ وَإِن تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: in tuʿadhdhibhum fa-innahum ʿibāduka wa-in taghfir lahum fa-innaka anta l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīm
versets.trad: If You punish them, they are Your servants; and if You forgive them, You are indeed the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Here is a summit of spiritual adab. Qushayrī marvels that ʿĪsā does not say "You are the Forgiving, the Compassionate," but "the Mighty, the Wise": he commits even the pardon to the sovereign wisdom, presuming nothing of the divine decision — for the perfect servant imposes nothing upon his Lord, not even mercy. All is suspended upon the good pleasure of the One whose might and wisdom ground all justice and all grace.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ع ب د
mots.sens: if You punish them, they are Your servants

mots.racine: غ ف ر
ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: قَالَ اللَّهُ هَٰذَا يَوْمُ يَنفَعُ الصَّادِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ ۚ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۚ رَّضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: qāla llāhu hādhā yawmu yanfaʿu ṣ-ṣādiqīna ṣidquhum lahum jannātun tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan raḍiya llāhu ʿanhum wa-raḍū ʿanhu dhālika l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: God said: This is the Day when the truthful will profit from their truthfulness. Theirs are Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is the supreme triumph.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings the sūra to its peak on mutual good pleasure (riḍā): "God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him" — here is the supreme triumph, where the Lord's contentment weds the servant's. And he notes that it is truthfulness (ṣidq) that opens this bliss: on the Day of unveiling, only the truth of the heart profits; all else vanishes.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / ص د ق
mots.sens: the truthful profit from their truth

mots.racine: ر ض و
mots.sens: God is pleased with them and they with Him

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā fīhinna wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and all that is in them, and He is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: The sūra seals itself with the affirmation of absolute kingship. Qushayrī: all returns to the One to whom all belongs; the heavens, the earth, and what they hold are but the places of manifestation of His power. To acknowledge that "all belongs to God" is the completion of the faithfulness to covenants with which the sūra opened: whoever has committed all to his Lord has nothing left to claim.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: to God the dominion of heavens and earth
and all that is in them

mots.racine: ق د ر
